sentier-geologique-pointe-percee-grand-b.meta_description: Aravis interpretive trail from the Col des Annes to the Refuge de la Pointe Percee: 4 geological stations, 10 landscape windows, free shuttles in summer.
sentier-geologique-pointe-percee-grand-b.hero_alt: Pointe Percee geological trail in the Aravis range from the Col des Annes, Le Grand-Bornand
sentier-geologique-pointe-percee-grand-b.body.what_is:
The Pointe Percée Geological Trail is a waymarked interpretive route across the Aravis range, from the green pastures of the Col des Annes (1,721 m) to the foot of the Pointe Percée (2,752 m), the highest peak of the chain. Designed to explain how the Northern Alps were formed — limestone strata, folding, glaciation and karst — it is dotted with 4 themed stations on Earth sciences plus around ten landscape interpretation windows.

The trail climbs via the Tête des Annes and the Col de l'Oulettaz to the Refuge de la Pointe Percée (FFCAM mountain hut, around 2,164 m), where the hike stops for general walkers; the summit itself is the domain of mountaineering. Expect around 6.9 km round trip, 2 h 30 to 3 h, around 540 m of ascent and a chain-assisted section just before the Col de l'Oulettaz: the trail is rated difficult. Le Grand-Bornand Tourisme operates free shuttles to the Col des Annes every summer, cutting traffic and allowing one-way loops. The route crosses pastures, rhododendron banks, glacial valleys and karst pavements and offers, on a clear day, an exceptional view of the Mont-Blanc massif.

sentier-morclan-chatel.name: Morclan Trail
sentier-morclan-chatel.meta_title: Morclan Trail - Chatel - Vallee d'Abondance | Loisirs 74
sentier-morclan-chatel.meta_description: Very easy interpretive trail at Chatel: 3.5 km, views over the Vallee d'Abondance and the Mont de Grange, open 15 June - 15 September.
sentier-morclan-chatel.hero_alt: Morclan trail at Chatel, alpine pastures with views over the Vallee d'Abondance and the Mont de Grange
sentier-morclan-chatel.body.what_is:
The Morclan Trail is a very easy interpretive route waymarked by the Châtel Tourist Office in the Vallée d'Abondance, within the Portes du Soleil ski area. About 3.5 km long, it takes around 2 h 30 to walk, climbing gently through the Vorraz, Louennaz and Sur l'Ortaz pastures from the Route du Petit-Châtel up to Super-Châtel.

The trail starts at the Virage du Tenne, at the end of the Route du Petit-Châtel (2 km from the village). It crosses a mixed forest before opening onto flower-strewn pastures, with a clear view of the sweeping arc of the Vallée d'Abondance and the Mont de Grange. Educational panels along the way introduce walkers to local fauna, flora and the mountain environment; marmots, chamois and alpine flowers are often spotted in season. The trail is open from 15 June to 15 September every day, weather permitting, and rated very easy — though the steady climb still calls for a bit of stamina.

sentier-oiseaux-chatel.name: Birds Trail
sentier-oiseaux-chatel.meta_title: Birds Trail - Col de Bassachaux - Chatel | Loisirs 74
sentier-oiseaux-chatel.meta_description: Ornithological interpretive trail at Chatel: from the Col de Bassachaux to the Rochassons summit on the GR5, in the Vallee d'Abondance (Chablais UNESCO Geopark).
sentier-oiseaux-chatel.hero_alt: Birds trail at Chatel from the Col de Bassachaux, in the Vallee d'Abondance
sentier-oiseaux-chatel.body.what_is:
The Birds Trail is an ornithological interpretive route along the section of the GR5 linking the Col de Bassachaux (1,778 m) to the Rochassons summit, in the commune of Châtel in the Vallée d'Abondance. It follows a wide driveable track throughout, ideal for families: an out-and-back walk, returning the same way.

Along the route, educational panels teach walkers how to identify the mountain birds seen around Châtel — crossbill, nutcracker, alpine chough, ptarmigan, common raven — alongside their habitats, diet and nesting habits. The Col de Bassachaux is reached by car via the D 228 from Châtel (12 km), with a large car park and a seasonal snack bar; it marks the end of the Vallée d'Abondance, beneath a sweeping panorama. The site is part of the Chablais UNESCO Global Geopark, whose history is bound up with the formation of the Northern Alps. The trail is usually open from 15 June to 15 September, weather permitting.

sentier-pedestre-a-decouverte-village-ve.name: Discovery Walking Trail of La Vernaz Village
sentier-pedestre-a-decouverte-village-ve.meta_title: La Vernaz Village Discovery Trail - Vallee d'Aulps | Loisirs 74
sentier-pedestre-a-decouverte-village-ve.meta_description: Easy interpretive trail at La Vernaz village: reading and drawing tables, forest walk, pink marble quarry, in the Vallee d'Aulps.
sentier-pedestre-a-decouverte-village-ve.hero_alt: Walking trail discovering La Vernaz village, Vallee d'Aulps
sentier-pedestre-a-decouverte-village-ve.body.what_is:
The Discovery Walking Trail of La Vernaz Village is an easy family walk laid out by the commune of La Vernaz, in the Vallée d'Aulps (Chablais), 15 min by car from Thonon-les-Bains. The trail starts at the town hall car park, where a welcome panel sets out the route, then follows a small tarmac road towards the hamlet of Plan de la Chaux.

Its charm lies in the reading tables and drawing tables dotted along the way: at every viewpoint or clearing, walkers can sit, learn to recognise local trees and work out their age, or take time to sketch the landscape. The trail then dives into the forest as far as the pink marble quarry of La Vernaz, a witness to the valley's geological past. It is an ideal outing for families, nature lovers, amateur sketchers and rural-heritage enthusiasts of the Chablais; the route is easy and presents no technical difficulty.

sentier-pedestre-eterlou-chatel.name: Eterlou Walking Trail
sentier-pedestre-eterlou-chatel.meta_description: Family interpretive trail at Pre-la-Joux (Chatel): 600 m loop, about 50 min, fauna and flora panels, open May - October.
sentier-pedestre-eterlou-chatel.hero_alt: Eterlou trail at Pre-la-Joux in Chatel, educational family loop
sentier-pedestre-eterlou-chatel.body.what_is:
The Eterlou Walking Trail is a fun, educational interpretive route at Pré-la-Joux, a high-altitude sector of Châtel at around 1,230 m, just beside the chairlift of the same name. Designed for families with young children, it forms a loop of about 0.6 km walked in around 50 minutes, with no technical difficulty: about 4 m of cumulative ascent and 15 m of descent.

Along this short route, around ten educational panels answer children's questions about the mountains: animal habitats, feeding habits, seasonal behaviour and risks (encountering a flock, crossing a stream, etc.). 'Eterlou' is the Savoyard dialect word for a young chamois, the trail's running theme. The path runs alongside the river coming down from Bassachaux: keep an eye on young children. In winter the site is shared with the toboggan runs and the snow play area of Pré-la-Joux. The trail is open from 1 May to 31 October, every day, weather permitting.

sentier-pedestre-interpretation-lac-plag.name: Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail
sentier-pedestre-interpretation-lac-plag.meta_title: Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail - Abondance | Loisirs 74
sentier-pedestre-interpretation-lac-plag.meta_description: Geological trail at the Lac des Plagnes (Abondance): lake loop, peat bog, erratic boulder, climb to Cubourre, Chablais UNESCO Global Geopark.
sentier-pedestre-interpretation-lac-plag.hero_alt: Interpretive trail around the Lac des Plagnes at Abondance, Vallee d'Abondance
sentier-pedestre-interpretation-lac-plag.body.what_is:
The Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail is an educational route around the Lac des Plagnes, a charming mountain lake at around 1,170 m altitude, in the commune of Abondance in the Vallée d'Abondance. The trail belongs to the Chablais UNESCO Global Geopark: its interpretive panels explain 20,000 years of landscape evolution, from the great Quaternary glaciers to today's slowly rising peat bogs.

The route begins with a loop around the lake on a flat, shaded path, then continues upstream along the lake's tributary, climbing through the forest beside a waterfall to the Cubourré plateau, drained by a small stream and dominated by the Pointe d'Entre Deux Pertuis and the Chavache. A wide track then leads to a mysterious erratic boulder whose origin is explained on site. The return is by the same direct path to the car park. Allow 2 to 3 hours depending on the option (lake loop alone, or lake loop plus Cubourré climb). Free access, swimming forbidden, a fine outing for families between geological heritage and nature observation.

sentier-pedestre-sens-et-decouverte-anim.name: Senses and Discovery of Farm Animals Walking Trail
sentier-pedestre-sens-et-decouverte-anim.meta_title: Farm Animal Discovery Trail - Ferme de Chosal - Copponex | Loisirs 74
sentier-pedestre-sens-et-decouverte-anim.meta_description: Ferme de Chosal trail at Copponex: observe donkeys, goats, pigs and other farm animals. Open 5 April - 3 November, EUR9.80 adult.
sentier-pedestre-sens-et-decouverte-anim.hero_alt: Farm animal discovery trail at the Ferme de Chosal, Copponex, Haute-Savoie
sentier-pedestre-sens-et-decouverte-anim.body.what_is:
The 'Senses and Discovery of Farm Animals' Walking Trail is one of the two flagship routes of the Ferme de Chosal, an educational farm at Copponex, in the French Genevois area, 15 min from Cruseilles. The trail invites visitors to observe and approach the farm animals in their enclosures, set in beautifully landscaped grounds.

Along the way visitors meet donkeys, sheep, goats, billy goats, pigs, hens, pheasants, guinea fowl, rabbits and guinea pigs. Panels describe the life, diet and behaviour of each species. The trail complements the barefoot 'Nature et Sens' sensory loop on the same site. The Ferme de Chosal is run by a workforce integration organisation that trains and employs people with disabilities: visits therefore also have a real social and inclusive dimension. The farm is open from 5 April to 3 November, 10 am to 6 pm (last entry 4.30 pm), with admission at €9.80 for adults and €7 for children; under-2s are free and reduced rates are available for groups, schools and visitors with disabilities.

sentier-pedestre-sonore-sur-traces-contr.name: Smugglers' Audio Walking Trail - Family Loop
sentier-pedestre-sonore-sur-traces-contr.meta_title: Smugglers' Audio Trail - Family Loop - Châtel | Loisirs 74
sentier-pedestre-sonore-sur-traces-contr.meta_description: Family audio trail at Châtel in the footsteps of the Chablais smugglers: 4 km, 2 h 30, start at the Vieille Douane, narrated by Victor.
sentier-pedestre-sonore-sur-traces-contr.hero_alt: Smugglers' audio trail at Châtel, starting at Lac de Vonnes and the Vieille Douane
sentier-pedestre-sonore-sur-traces-contr.body.what_is:
The Smugglers' Audio Walking Trail - Family Loop is an audio-guided walk laid out by the Châtel Tourist Office, in the Vallée d'Abondance. Equipped with a paper topoguide and a smartphone (or a rentable audio-guide), you follow the border paths between France and Switzerland once used by smugglers to slip salt, tobacco, coffee, chocolate and cheese across the line, and by the gabelous (customs officers) chasing them.

The family loop starts at Lac de Vonnes, in front of the Vieille Douane (Mountain Smuggling Interpretation Centre). It forms a 4 km loop with about 200 m of climb, walked in 2 h 30 with listening stops. A character - Victor, a former smuggler - invites walkers to eavesdrop on his accomplices telling their adventures; children follow the plot from one stop to the next. A second, longer and tougher loop completes the offer for keen adult walkers.

After the walk, the Mountain Smuggling Interpretation Centre inside the Vieille Douane extends the experience with a museum visit.

sentier-renard-morzine.name: The Fox Trail
sentier-renard-morzine.meta_title: Fox Trail - Morzine - Vallée de la Dranse | Loisirs 74
sentier-renard-morzine.meta_description: Nature walk at Morzine along the Dranse towards the Nyon alpine pastures: 2 h, panels on the fox, wildlife, flora and geology. Snowshoe-friendly in winter.
sentier-renard-morzine.hero_alt: Fox Trail at Morzine along the Dranse, Nyon alpine pastures
sentier-renard-morzine.body.what_is:
The Fox Trail is an emblematic nature walk at Morzine, in the Vallée d'Aulps. The route follows the old way up to the Nyon alpine pastures, along the Dranse, and offers an introduction to the life of the fox and its role in the ecosystem, the riverside habitat (otter, trout, riparian woodland) and the geology of the Aulps valley.

From Morzine church, you cross the old bridge, pass the library to reach rue Bidon, then drop under the road via a footbridge and follow the path along the Dranse. Educational panels line the route, covering wildlife, flora and the riverside environment. Allow about 2 hours return and 160 m of climb to reach the Cascade de Nyon, or about 4 hours and 420 m of climb if you carry on to Lac des Mines d'Or from the Nyon car park.

The path stays wide and gently sloped, suiting the whole family; in winter, it can be snowshoed. Free access year-round.

stelsia-casino-megeve.name: Stelsia Casino Megève
stelsia-casino-megeve.meta_title: Stelsia Casino Megève - slot machines & table games | Loisirs 74
stelsia-casino-megeve.meta_description: Stelsia Casino in the heart of Megève village: 65 slot machines, electronic roulette, traditional table games, Gallery Bar and Le Rendez-vous nightclub.
stelsia-casino-megeve.hero_alt: Stelsia Casino Megève in the heart of the village, 192 rue Charles Feige
stelsia-casino-megeve.body.what_is:
The Stelsia Casino Megève is one of Haute-Savoie's two casinos on the Mont-Blanc side and the only casino in this Savoyard village. Located at 192 rue Charles Feige, right in the centre, it belongs to the Le Stelsia Casinos group, which runs several venues across France.

The complex offers 65 latest-generation slot machines (stakes from €0.01 to €5.00), electronic roulette, traditional games (English roulette, blackjack, seasonal poker), a VIP room, plus the atmospheric Gallery Bar and the Le Rendez-vous nightclub to round off the evening. The casino also hosts occasional exhibitions and themed events.

Access to the gaming room is free of charge but requires a valid ID and appropriate dress; entry is forbidden to under-18s, people on the gaming-exclusion register and pets. Opening hours vary by season (extended to around 4 a.m. in the high winter season, reduced in shoulder season) - check the official website for the day's schedule.

suivez-mouche-alby-sur-cheran.name: Follow the Fly
suivez-mouche-alby-sur-cheran.meta_title: Follow the Fly - Alby-sur-Chéran - village trail | Loisirs 74
suivez-mouche-alby-sur-cheran.meta_description: 2 km / 1 h village trail at Alby-sur-Chéran: follow the flies on the ground and discover the cobblers' heritage, the Chéran river and the medieval town.
suivez-mouche-alby-sur-cheran.hero_alt: Follow the Fly trail in the village of Alby-sur-Chéran, cobblers' fly motifs on the ground
suivez-mouche-alby-sur-cheran.body.what_is:
"Follow the Fly" is a self-guided discovery trail through the village of Alby-sur-Chéran, a former medieval market town famous for its cobblers, 15 km south of Annecy. It runs to 2 km and about 1 hour on foot, walked solo, with family or with friends, starting from the Place du Trophée. Visitors follow the small flies drawn on the ground through 12 stops in the lanes, along the Chéran river and at the foot of the ramparts.

Why a fly? The nail the cobblers used to fix under shoe soles had a head reminiscent of fly wings, and the Chéran is famous for its fly-fishers. At each stop, walkers can answer a question whose answer hides a few paces away. When the Cobblers' Museum (Musée de la Cordonnerie) is open, children can join a hunt for "Saint Crispin's hammer" (patron saint of cobblers).

An audio tour in French and English, narrated by the Chéran itself, is also available on izi.TRAVEL. The trail is free year-round and makes an ideal heritage stop on a tour around Lake Annecy.

tactiq-aventure-cruseilles.name: Tactiq Aventure
tactiq-aventure-cruseilles.meta_title: Tactiq Aventure - Parc des Dronières - Cruseilles | Loisirs 74
tactiq-aventure-cruseilles.meta_description: Tree-top adventure park at Cruseilles in the Dronières forest: 3 courses (Discovery / Junior / Vertige) from 90 cm, Tactiq Game escape forest, open April to Nov.
tactiq-aventure-cruseilles.hero_alt: Tactiq Aventure tree-top adventure park in the Dronières forest at Cruseilles
tactiq-aventure-cruseilles.body.what_is:
Tactiq Aventure is the tree-top adventure park inside the Parc des Dronières at Cruseilles, halfway between Annecy and Geneva. Set in a fine deciduous forest at 785 m altitude, it offers three tiered courses from 90 cm in height, plus an adventure-in-the-forest game called Tactiq Game.

All three courses use a continuous lifeline system: the Discovery course (from 90 cm, €16) is for children aged 4-5 and up; the Junior course (from 1.15 m, €20) adds height and ziplines for ages 7-12; the Vertige course (from 1.40 m, €25 adult / €22 under-16) features long ziplines, freefall jumps and aerial passages for teens and adults. Tactiq Game (€10) is an escape-forest experience blending riddles and observation, a good warm-up or add-on.

The park is open from 4 April to 1 November, with a seasonal calendar: daily 1.30 p.m.-6.30 p.m. in April, Wednesdays, weekends and bank holidays 1.30 p.m.-6.30 p.m. until early July, then daily 10 a.m.-7 p.m. from 4 July to 30 August. Booking strongly recommended in high season.

thiou-a-annecy-annecy.name: The Thiou River in Annecy
thiou-a-annecy-annecy.meta_title: The Thiou in Annecy - canals and heritage walk | Loisirs 74
thiou-a-annecy-annecy.meta_description: The Thiou, the outflow of Lake Annecy: 3.5 km through the old town, 62 Sadi Carnot floodgates, Pont des Amours, Palais de l'Île, heritage walk.
thiou-a-annecy-annecy.hero_alt: The Thiou crossing the old town of Annecy, seen from the Pont des Amours
thiou-a-annecy-annecy.body.what_is:
The Thiou is a short 3.5 km river and the natural outflow of Lake Annecy, joining the Fier at Meythet (which in turn flows into the Rhône). As it leaves the lake, it splits into two main arms - Le Port and the Vassé canal - which cross the historic heart of Annecy before rejoining downstream of the Palais de l'Île. With an average flow of 8 m³/s, it is both the city's scenic axis and its historic backbone.

The Thiou is the spine of the old quarter, designated Ville d'Art et d'Histoire: its quaysides, arcades, Pont des Amours, flower-lined footbridges and the emblematic Palais de l'Île (12th century) have become Annecy's postcard image. The river also has an industrial past: the Aussedat paper mill, the Cran forges, tanneries and printing works ran on its waterpower in the 19th century.

After Savoy's union with France (1860), engineer Sadi Carnot redesigned the canals and in 1874 installed a clever system of 62 floodgates over 6 km, which still regulate the lake's level today. A heritage walk mapped out by the City of Annecy can be followed on foot, free year-round.

tour-village-abondance-abondance.name: Tour of Abondance Village
tour-village-abondance-abondance.meta_title: Tour of Abondance Village - Vallée d'Abondance | Loisirs 74
tour-village-abondance-abondance.meta_description: Very easy 1 h 30 family walk at Abondance: village centre, abbey, Maison du Fromage and a loop along the Dranse. Map available at the Tourist Office.
tour-village-abondance-abondance.hero_alt: Tour of Abondance village with view of the bell tower and the Vallée d'Abondance
tour-village-abondance-abondance.body.what_is:
The Tour of Abondance Village is a very easy family walk that takes about 1 h 30 to discover the historic town of Abondance, its heritage and the surrounding nature. The loop starts from the village centre and first follows the path along the Dranse. It then climbs gently through woodland on the slopes above Abondance to a viewpoint with an open panorama over the village, its mountains and the bell tower.

The route passes close to the Abbaye d'Abondance (12th century), a Cistercian site open daily in summer, and the Maison du Fromage Abondance, where you can stop to discover the making of Abondance PDO pressed-cooked cheese and the diversity of Savoyard cheeses. From there, a forest track along the banks of the Dranse leads back to the village centre.

The route is waymarked in yellow (PDIPR); a map is available at the tourist information office. Dogs must be kept on a lead because of the grazing herds.

accrobranche-foret-aventures-manigod.name: Forêt d'Aventures de Manigod
accrobranche-foret-aventures-manigod.meta_description: Treetop adventure park at the Col de la Croix Fry (1,477 m) in Manigod, in the Aravis range: trails for ages 4 up, facing the range and Lake Annecy.
accrobranche-foret-aventures-manigod.hero_alt: Local Aravis treetop adventure park Forêt d'Aventures de Manigod at Manigod, Haute-Savoie 74
accrobranche-foret-aventures-manigod.body.what_is:
The Forêt d'Aventures de Manigod is a treetop adventure park perched at the Col de la Croix Fry, at 1,477 m altitude in the Aravis range. Ziplines run above the alpine pastures, with trails suited to the whole family (children from age 4, intermediate and sporty levels) and an exceptional panorama of the Aravis range with Lake Annecy in the background.

The park is the official local recommendation under the 'Indian Forest Annecy-Les Aravis Manigod' label. The Indian Forest network has no other site in the Haute-Savoie department, so this Manigod park is the reference for the area, in a mid-mountain setting overlooking the Aravis.

2026 prices: adult £20 / €20, child €15 (ages 4–12). Season: May to October. Weekends and bank holidays 10am–6pm in May–June and September–October, daily 9am–7pm in July and August. Not suitable for reduced-mobility visitors (high-ropes trails).

acroparc-de-bellavallis-bellevaux.name: Acroparc de Bellavallis
acroparc-de-bellavallis-bellevaux.meta_title: Acroparc de Bellavallis · Bellevaux · 2026 prices · Treetop park
acroparc-de-bellavallis-bellevaux.meta_description: Treetop adventure park at Bellevaux (74): 8 trails, 70 workshops, ziplines from age 3. Children's island €10, ages 6–16 €17, adult €23. May to October.
acroparc-de-bellavallis-bellevaux.hero_alt: Zipline and treetop course in the forest of the Acroparc de Bellavallis adventure park at Bellevaux
acroparc-de-bellavallis-bellevaux.body.what_is:
The Acroparc de Bellavallis is a treetop adventure park set at 950 m altitude at the foot of the Cascade de la Diomaz, in the commune of Bellevaux in the Brevon valley (Haut-Chablais). Run by GR Aventure, it offers 8 trails and 70 workshops organised by level and age, with many ziplines threading through the forest.

The park is divided into three zones: the Île aux enfants from age 3 (low-height trails, no minimum size, €10), the children's trails for ages 6–16 (min. 1.10 m, €17) and the adult trails for ages 16 and up (€23). Each ticket gives access to the trails suited to the participant's age and size, and every trail can be repeated several times.

GR Aventure also rents out via ferrata equipment to tackle the nearby Via Ferrata de la Diomaz (from age 12, 1.40 m, 40–120 kg): full kit without pulley €20, with pulley €25. The park is open from April to October, closed from 1 November to 30 April.

aquaparc-thonon-piscine-olympique-thonon.name: Olympic Pool of Thonon
aquaparc-thonon-piscine-olympique-thonon.meta_description: 50-metre Olympic outdoor pool on the shore of Lake Geneva at Thonon-les-Bains, with diving boards, beach and children's pool. Major public facility, adult entry €4.
aquaparc-thonon-piscine-olympique-thonon.hero_alt: Olympic outdoor 50m pool + Lake Geneva beach at the Olympic Pool of Thonon in Thonon-les-Bains, Haute-Savoie 74
aquaparc-thonon-piscine-olympique-thonon.body.what_is:
The Olympic Pool of Thonon is a large aquatic facility set right on the shore of Lake Geneva at Thonon-les-Bains. It combines a 50-metre Olympic outdoor pool, a children's pool and several diving boards, doubled with a beach facing the lake. A major public facility of Thonon Agglomération, it opens for the summer season, from May to September.

The complex offers direct access to the Lake Geneva shoreline, so swimmers can switch between the pool and open-water bathing in the lake itself, with a view across to the Vaud Alps on the Swiss side.

2026 prices: adult €4, child €2.80. Season: May to September (outdoor season). Hours: daily 10am–7pm, with variations depending on the weather. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

base-de-loisirs-du-lac-aux-dames.name: Lac aux Dames Leisure Park
base-de-loisirs-du-lac-aux-dames.meta_title: Lac aux Dames Leisure Park · Full guide (Samoëns)
base-de-loisirs-du-lac-aux-dames.meta_description: 10-hectare leisure park around two lakes at Samoëns. Supervised swimming in summer, playgrounds, sports pitches, fishing, paddle. Free access.
base-de-loisirs-du-lac-aux-dames.hero_alt: Lac aux Dames leisure park at Samoëns with its two lakes and green spaces in Haute-Savoie
base-de-loisirs-du-lac-aux-dames.body.what_is:
The Lac aux Dames Leisure Park is a 10-hectare leisure area at Samoëns, built around two adjoining lakes. Set away from the village centre, it offers a calm, safe environment, away from car traffic, ideal for families and groups of friends. The park is open all year and free to enter.

The site offers a wide range of activities: supervised swimming in the lake from early July to early September (12pm–6pm), a children's playground, sports pitches (football, basketball, beach volley, pétanque), fishing, lakeside walks, stand-up paddle and a discovery trail. Paying facilities are also available: an open-air swimming pool with slides and three basins, tennis and padel courts, an adventure course and paragliding.

The park includes the Espace Le Bois aux Dames, a cultural and sports venue with a 426-seat hall (1,100 standing) hosting concerts, plays and sporting events. Two bar-restaurants and picnic areas with toilets round out the offer, and the park is the starting point for family hikes along the Giffre and the inter-lake trail to Morillon.

base-de-loisirs-du-vuaz-filliere.name: Vuaz Leisure Area
base-de-loisirs-du-vuaz-filliere.meta_title: Vuaz Leisure Area · Aviernoz · Fillière · Free
base-de-loisirs-du-vuaz-filliere.meta_description: Free public leisure area open year-round at Aviernoz (Fillière), at the foot of the Parmelan: children's playground, picnic tables, barbecue. Pets welcome.
base-de-loisirs-du-vuaz-filliere.hero_alt: Playground and picnic tables at the free Vuaz leisure area at Aviernoz, at the foot of the Parmelan
base-de-loisirs-du-vuaz-filliere.body.what_is:
The Vuaz Leisure Area is a free public leisure space managed by the commune of Fillière, located in the hamlet of Le Vuaz at Aviernoz, a short distance from the village centre of Thorens-Glières, at the foot of the Parmelan massif. The site borders the Fillière river and offers a peaceful natural setting 13 km north-east of Annecy.

Confirmed facilities include a children's playground with swings, climbing frames and slides, picnic tables, a free-access barbecue and a pétanque court. The leisure area also lists other items (a zipline for ages 5–15, a ping-pong table, toddler games) which are worth checking on site. Parking is free on Route de la Louvatière, and pets are welcome.

Aviernoz is one of the five historic villages that merged on 1 January 2017 to form the new commune of Fillière (with Évires, Les Ollières, Saint-Martin-Bellevue and Thorens-Glières). The village keeps its identity and its postal code 74570. The leisure area is open all year, freely accessible 24/7, with no ticketing or set opening hours.

base-nautique-doussard-doussard.name: Doussard Watersports Centre
base-nautique-doussard-doussard.meta_description: Watersports centre at the southern port of Lake Annecy at Doussard: sailing, wakeboard and paddle hosted with Skiwake74 and other operators, reached via the Voie verte.
base-nautique-doussard-doussard.hero_alt: Multi-operator watersports centre – sailing + wake + paddle at the Doussard Watersports Centre, Haute-Savoie 74
base-nautique-doussard-doussard.body.what_is:
The Doussard Watersports Centre occupies the southern port of Lake Annecy, on the Doussard / Bout-du-Lac shore. Several private operators share the infrastructure: Skiwake74 for wakeboard and water-skiing, sailing schools and paddle rentals. The southern shore is directly linked to the lakeside Voie verte cycle path, making the site a practical starting point for water sports.

The centre covers all main disciplines: sailing (Optimist, dinghy), towed wakeboard, water-skiing, stand-up paddle (SUP) and kayak. The flat water of the early morning makes the spot particularly suited to technical disciplines and beginner courses, while the southern end of the lake gives easy onshore access via the Voie verte.

2026 prices: adult €30–80 depending on activity, child courses for ages 6–15. Season: May to September. Hours: rentals and lessons 9am–7pm in summer season. Partially accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

base-nautique-marquisats-annecy.name: Marquisats Watersports Centre
base-nautique-marquisats-annecy.meta_description: At the foot of Annecy's Old Town, the Marquisats watersports centre brings together the SRVA and the Club des Sports Nautiques: sailing, rowing, paddle on the lake.
base-nautique-marquisats-annecy.hero_alt: Sailing school + rowing + paddle – SRVA + Sports Nautiques – Marquisats Watersports Centre in Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
base-nautique-marquisats-annecy.body.what_is:
The Marquisats Watersports Centre, in the centre of Annecy, brings together two emblematic clubs: the SRVA (Société des Régates à Voile d'Annecy), the historic sailing club, and the Club des Sports Nautiques (rowing, kayak, paddle). Its central beach at the foot of the Old Town makes it a particularly well-placed venue for beginner lessons, week-long courses and training sessions.

The centre covers all watersports: sailing (Optimist, Laser, catamaran), rowing, kayak and stand-up paddle (SUP). Children's, teenage and adult courses run through the summer season, and equipment is also available to rent. The Marquisats beach is open to the public and offers direct access to Lake Annecy from the city centre.

2026 prices: adult €30–60 depending on activity, children's courses available. Season: March to October. Hours: lessons and rentals according to schedule – check the website. Partially accessible for reduced-mobility visitors (with adapted sailing).

base-nautique-sciez-sciez.name: Voile Léman Sciez Watersports Centre
base-nautique-sciez-sciez.meta_description: Multi-discipline Lake Geneva sailing centre at Sciez: kitesurfing, dinghy and catamaran on the south shore, with optimal thermal winds in the afternoon.
base-nautique-sciez-sciez.hero_alt: Lake Geneva sailing centre – kite, dinghy and catamaran – Voile Léman Sciez at Sciez, Haute-Savoie 74
base-nautique-sciez-sciez.body.what_is:
The Voile Léman Sciez Watersports Centre is a multi-discipline Lake Geneva sailing centre based at Sciez-Plage (74140), on the southern shore of Lake Geneva. The location is renowned for its optimal thermal winds in the afternoon (Vaudaire, Bornan), making it an ideal spot for sailing and windsurfing.

The school offers a wide range of activities: kitesurfing (approved school), dinghy sailing (Optimist for children, Laser and 420 for teens and adults), catamaran (Hobie Cat 15/16) and windsurfing. Five-day courses run all summer for all levels, with private lessons and equipment hire also available.

2026 prices: adult courses €200–400 depending on discipline, children's courses available. Season: May to September. Hours: daily 9am–6pm in season; out of season by reservation. Partially accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

bowling-aerodrome-annemasse.name: Bowling de l'Aérodrome
bowling-aerodrome-annemasse.meta_description: Bowling de l'Aérodrome at Annemasse: 12 pool tables, arcade games, restaurant and panoramic terrace with views of Mont Blanc, a few minutes from Geneva.
bowling-aerodrome-annemasse.hero_alt: Bowling – 12 pool tables + games + restaurant – Bowling de l'Aérodrome at Annemasse, Haute-Savoie 74
bowling-aerodrome-annemasse.body.what_is:
The Bowling de l'Aérodrome is a leisure complex at Annemasse, right next to the local airfield. It features twelve American and French pool tables, a video-games area (pinball, arcade, simulators) and a restaurant paired with a panoramic terrace overlooking Mont Blanc. The venue is easily reached from Geneva and is well suited to family evenings or group outings.

The restaurant and the panoramic terrace are a strong point of the venue, with classic brasserie cuisine and a particularly enjoyable view of the Mont-Blanc range at sunset. The bowling lanes, pool tables and arcade games make the complex a one-stop evening venue, combining game, drink and meal under one roof.

2026 prices: adult €8 per game, child €6. Season: year-round. Hours: Mon–Thu 2pm–1am, Fri–Sat 2pm–3am, Sun 2pm–midnight; school holidays open from noon. Partially accessible for reduced-mobility visitors (ground floor).

bowling-le-bowl-annecy.name: Le Bowl Bowling Annecy
bowling-le-bowl-annecy.meta_description: 16 lanes + restaurant + nightclub – evening leisure complex in central Annecy. Combined night out: bowling, dinner and club in one venue.
bowling-le-bowl-annecy.hero_alt: Bowling 16 lanes + restaurant + nightclub – Le Bowl Bowling Annecy at Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
bowling-le-bowl-annecy.body.what_is:
Le Bowl Bowling Annecy is a leisure complex in central Annecy combining 16 bowling lanes, a restaurant and a nightclub. The unusual mix of bowling, dining and club venue under one roof makes it a one-stop evening destination, particularly popular with groups, students and night-out crowds.

The complex is located at 9 boulevard du Fier in Annecy, on the main axis linking the Old Town to the commercial activity zone. It is reachable on foot from the city centre, with free parking along the boulevard, making it accessible for visitors and locals alike.

2026 prices: adult €8 per game, child €6. Season: year-round. Hours: Mon–Thu 2pm–1am, Fri–Sat 2pm–4am, Sun 2pm–midnight. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors (ground floor).

bowling-margencel-margencel.name: Bowling de Margencel
bowling-margencel-margencel.meta_description: 20 bowling lanes + restaurant – the largest bowling alley near Thonon. 10 minutes from Thonon, ideal for an evening out in the Bas-Chablais.
bowling-margencel-margencel.hero_alt: Bowling 20 lanes + restaurant – Bowling de Margencel at Margencel, Haute-Savoie 74
bowling-margencel-margencel.body.what_is:
The Bowling de Margencel is a leisure venue at Margencel (74200), in the Bas-Chablais. With 20 lanes it is the largest bowling alley near Thonon-les-Bains — only 10 minutes from the town centre — and is easily reached from the whole Bas-Chablais area and the southern shore of Lake Geneva.

The venue includes a traditional restaurant open at lunch and dinner, ideal for family or group evenings combining a meal with several games of bowling. The cuisine is classic brasserie style, designed for everyday dining and group bookings.

2026 prices: adult €8 per game, child €6. Season: year-round. Hours: Mon–Thu 4pm–1am, Fri–Sat 2pm–3am, Sun 2pm–11pm; school holidays open from noon. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors (ground floor).

bowling-sevrier-sevrier.name: Bowling International d'Annecy-Sevrier
bowling-sevrier-sevrier.meta_description: Bowling International d'Annecy-Sevrier: 10 lanes and a restaurant on the west shore of Lake Annecy, route d'Albertville at Sevrier — alternative to Le Bowl Annecy.
bowling-sevrier-sevrier.hero_alt: Bowling – 10 lanes + restaurant – Bowling International d'Annecy-Sevrier at Sevrier, Haute-Savoie 74
bowling-sevrier-sevrier.body.what_is:
The Bowling International d'Annecy-Sevrier has ten lanes paired with a restaurant. It is set on the route d'Albertville at Sevrier, on the western shore of Lake Annecy. The venue serves as an alternative to Le Bowl Annecy for residents and visitors on the southern half of the lake, particularly around Sevrier and Saint-Jorioz.

The on-site restaurant is a traditional brasserie, well suited to family evenings combining a meal with a few games. A bar and lounge area completes the offer, providing a space to wait between games or to round off the evening.

2026 prices: adult €8, child €6. Season: year-round. Hours: Tue–Thu 4pm–1am, Fri–Sat 4pm–3am, Sun 2pm–11pm; closed Monday. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

c5-kids-party-ville-la-grand.name: C5 Kids Party
c5-kids-party-ville-la-grand.meta_title: C5 Kids Party · Ville-la-Grand · 2026 prices · Children's park
c5-kids-party-ville-la-grand.meta_description: Indoor play park for children aged 0–12 at Ville-la-Grand near Annemasse: 1,200 m², karting, indoor sledge run. Ages 4–12 €13, 1–3 €8, under 1 free.
c5-kids-party-ville-la-grand.hero_alt: Multi-level play structure and indoor sledge run at the C5 Kids Party children's park at Ville-la-Grand
c5-kids-party-ville-la-grand.body.what_is:
C5 Kids Party is an indoor play park for children aged 0 to 12, located at Ville-la-Grand (Haute-Savoie), a few minutes from Annemasse and the Swiss border, opposite the Leroy Merlin store. Charlie, the venue's mascot, welcomes families across 1,200 m² of covered, air-conditioned and secure space.

The park is divided into two worlds: a zone for ages 0–3 with sensory games, a ball pool, soft modules and a motor-skills course; and a large 4–12 zone with giant slides, suspension bridges, a maze, an adventure course and mini football and basketball pitches. The headline attractions are electric karting, a 15-metre indoor sledge run, arcade games, trampolines, a Power Tower and a zipline.

Simple day-rate pricing: €13 for ages 4–12, €8 for ages 1–3, free for under 1. Accompanying adults €2. Reduced rate for GIA Annemasse holders. Personalised birthday parties available in three packages from €15.90 to €19.90 per child.

casino-evian-resort-evian.name: Casino Évian Resort
casino-evian-resort-evian.meta_description: Historic Belle Époque casino at Évian on the shore of Lake Geneva, Quai Charles-Albert-Besson: slot machines, gaming tables and exceptional architectural heritage.
casino-evian-resort-evian.hero_alt: Historic Belle Époque gaming casino – Casino Évian Resort at Évian-les-Bains, Haute-Savoie 74
casino-evian-resort-evian.body.what_is:
The Casino Évian Resort, at Évian-les-Bains, is a historic casino founded in the late 19th century in the Belle Époque style, set on the shore of Lake Geneva at Quai Charles-Albert-Besson. Its exceptional architectural heritage is an integral part of the town's spa identity, and it houses both slot machines and gaming tables in a prestigious setting.

The casino is part of the Évian Resort group (formerly Royal Resort), a key heritage destination on the French side of Lake Geneva. The slot floor and table games occupy elegant Belle-Époque rooms that double as venues for events and entertainment throughout the year.

2026 prices: adult free entry, ages 18+ with ID; under-18s not admitted. Season: year-round. Hours: slot machine room 10am–3am; table game room 8.30pm–3am. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

casino-imperial-palace-annecy.name: Casino Impérial Palace Annecy
casino-imperial-palace-annecy.meta_description: Casino Impérial Palace at 32 avenue d'Albigny in Annecy, on the lakeside: slot machines, roulette, blackjack and poker, 5-star hotel and gourmet restaurant.
casino-imperial-palace-annecy.hero_alt: Gaming casino + 5-star hotel + restaurant – Casino Impérial Palace Annecy at Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
casino-imperial-palace-annecy.body.what_is:
The Casino Impérial Palace Annecy stands at 32 avenue d'Albigny in Annecy-le-Vieux, on the lakeside facing the Plage d'Albigny. A prestige establishment, it combines a slot-machine room, gaming tables (roulette, blackjack, stud poker, Texas Hold'em), an integrated 5-star hotel and a gourmet restaurant. Free admission for over-18s on production of ID.

The slot-machine room is open daily 10am–3am, while the table-game room opens every evening 9pm–3am. The combination of casino, palatial hotel and lakeside setting makes the Impérial Palace one of the landmark addresses of Annecy's belle-époque waterfront.

2026 prices: adult free entry, ages 18+ with ID – games priced by stake; under-18s not admitted. Season: year-round. Hours: slot machines 10am–3am daily; table games 9pm–3am. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

chateau-avully-brenthonne.name: Château d'Avully
chateau-avully-brenthonne.meta_description: 14th-century fortified manor restored by the Guyon family since 1971: water-filled moat, drawbridge, medieval wall paintings, escape game. Adult €10.
chateau-avully-brenthonne.hero_alt: Château d'Avully at Brenthonne with its water-filled moat, restored drawbridge and 14th-century medieval façade restored by the Guyon family, Haute-Savoie
chateau-avully-brenthonne.body.what_is:
The Château d'Avully at Brenthonne (74890) — also known as the Château Saint-Michel d'Avully, after the seigneurial family who held it from the 13th to the 18th century — is one of the most affecting castles in the Chablais. A fortified manor of the 13th–14th centuries, strengthened in the 14th on the order of the Count of Savoy, it lived through the Protestant Reformation, was confiscated at the Revolution and gradually fell into ruin in the 19th century. Listed as a Monument Historique in 1974 (Mérimée notice PA00118354).

Since 1971, the Guyon family has carried out an exceptional heritage restoration that continues to this day, over more than 50 years. The water-filled moat has been cleared, the drawbridge rebuilt using medieval techniques, the keep restored, and rare medieval wall paintings rediscovered and conserved. 18th-century-style gardens have been recreated from period plans and several rooms refitted for events. The work has been awarded several heritage prizes.

2026 prices: adult €10, student/jobseeker €8, child 5–12 €5, free under 5. Guided tour supplement €2. Medieval escape game €18/person, seasonal and by booking. 2026 opening: visits all year by appointment; from 1 July to 31 August, Monday–Friday 1.30pm–6pm without appointment, weekends by booking. Bed-and-breakfast and venue hire available. Summer festivals (early music, medieval theatre). Partial wheelchair access. Free parking.

chateau-beauregard-saint-jeoire.name: Château de Beauregard
chateau-beauregard-saint-jeoire.meta_title: Château de Beauregard · Saint-Jeoire · FREE · Illuminated by night
chateau-beauregard-saint-jeoire.meta_description: 12th–13th-c. fortified manor, held by the La Fléchère family for 8 centuries, illuminated at night. Exterior free, JEP visits. Eucharistein community. Saint-Jeoire 74.
chateau-beauregard-saint-jeoire.hero_alt: Rectangular 12th-century keep of the Château de Beauregard illuminated at night in the village of Saint-Jeoire-en-Faucigny, Haute-Savoie
chateau-beauregard-saint-jeoire.body.what_is:
The Château de Beauregard at Saint-Jeoire-en-Faucigny (74490) is one of the most evocative fortified manors of the Faucigny. A 12th–13th-century fortified manor, it was owned by the La Fléchère family for 8 centuries — an exceptional case of family continuity in Savoyard heritage. The original rectangular keep, with walls 1.20 metres thick, is typical of Faucigny Savoyard military architecture.

The castle had an eventful history: burnt in 1589 during the Bernese invasion (Reformation), restored in the 17th–18th centuries; burnt again during the French Revolution, restored in the 19th. Given to the diocese of Annecy in 2004, the château was entrusted in 2008 to the Fraternité Eucharistein, a mixed Catholic community founded in 1973 (monastic life, spiritual guesthouse, retreats). The interior cannot be visited outside the European Heritage Days in mid-September.

The standout feature is that the château is illuminated at night — a lighting scheme that highlights the walls, the keep and the approaches, visible from the D907 road and the village of Saint-Jeoire. It is one of the few castles in Haute-Savoie to offer a free, unsupervised night-time approach, particularly striking in winter. Free admission to the surroundings year-round; interior only during the European Heritage Days. Car recommended (no direct public transport), A40 Bonneville exit 15 minutes away. Leashed pets welcome.

chateau-bellegarde-thonon.name: Château de Bellegarde
chateau-bellegarde-thonon.meta_title: Château de Bellegarde · Thonon · FREE · Porte des Lombards
chateau-bellegarde-thonon.meta_description: Last remnant of Thonon's medieval fortifications: Porte des Lombards, square tower, 19th-century Rabelaisian friezes. Exteriors free, JEP visits in September.
chateau-bellegarde-thonon.hero_alt: Medieval square tower of the Château de Bellegarde guarding the Porte des Lombards, last remnant of the fortifications of Thonon-les-Bains, Haute-Savoie
chateau-bellegarde-thonon.body.what_is:
The Château de Bellegarde at Thonon-les-Bains (74200) is the last surviving fragment of the town's medieval fortifications. Originally a square tower set into the city walls, it once guarded the Porte des Lombards, the northern gate of the fortified medieval town. It stands centrally on the Place de l'Hôtel de Ville, two minutes' walk from the Tourist Office, the Musée du Chablais (Château de Sonnaz) and the Port de Rives.

The name 'Bellegarde' comes from Janus Bellegarde, syndic and secretary of the syndic of Thonon in the 16th century, who lived in the tower. The château today houses Thonon's law courts (district court and commercial court), so the interior is not open outside the European Heritage Days in mid-September. Those days are also the only chance to see the castle's hidden treasure: remarkable 19th-century Rabelaisian friezes in carved wood — bawdy, comic scenes inspired by Rabelais, an exceptional example of heritage wood carving in the region.

Free admission to the exterior year-round; interior only during the European Heritage Days. The site is in the pedestrian zone, with town-centre car parks nearby. Star't bus lines A and B stop 'Hôtel de Ville' is 2 minutes away, with Thonon Léman Express station 1 km off. A natural combination: the exterior of the Château de Bellegarde, the Musée du Chablais and the Château de Ripaille make a full heritage day in Thonon.

chateau-chatillon-sur-cluses.name: Château de Châtillon-sur-Cluses
chateau-chatillon-sur-cluses.meta_title: Château de Châtillon-sur-Cluses · FREE · Ruins on the col, 862 m
chateau-chatillon-sur-cluses.meta_description: Ruins of a 10th–12th-c. castle, residence of the Sires of Faucigny before Bonneville. Col at 862 m, 15–20 min trail. Free access year-round, restoration in progress.
chateau-chatillon-sur-cluses.hero_alt: Ruins of the two tower bases and curtain wall of the Château de Châtillon-sur-Cluses on the col at 862 m altitude, Haute-Savoie
chateau-chatillon-sur-cluses.body.what_is:
The Château de Châtillon-sur-Cluses is one of the most evocative and overlooked heritage sites of the Faucigny: ruins of a 10th–12th-century castle perched on the Col de Châtillon at 862 m altitude. The strategic position controlled access between the Arve valley (Cluses, below to the east) and the Giffre valley (Taninges and the Haut-Giffre, to the west). The castle was the preferred residence of the Sires of Faucigny before the Château de Bonneville was built around 1260.

What survives today is two cylindrical tower bases, a partly preserved curtain wall running for some 50 metres, and the adjoining Saint-Maurice church (Romanesque, more modest than the castle itself). The site is partly collapsed after centuries of neglect following the Sires' move to Bonneville. A restoration project is currently being prepared by the commune of Châtillon-sur-Cluses and the Communauté de Communes Faucigny-Glières: way-marking, interpretation panels, securing of the ruins, schedule still to be confirmed.

Free admission year-round, open access. Marked trail of 15–20 minutes from the village centre of Châtillon-sur-Cluses, with a moderate 80 m climb. The path is earthen — sturdy trainers recommended. Car advisable (no direct public transport), A40 Cluses exit 10 minutes away, free village parking. Leashed pets welcome. NOT wheelchair accessible (sloping path). Can be combined with Cluses, the Col de la Ramaz 8 km away, or the Désert de Platé.

chateau-clermont-genevois.name: Château de Clermont
chateau-clermont-genevois.meta_description: Rare Italian Renaissance château (1576–1580) in Savoy. Three levels of arcaded galleries, listed MH 1950. Adult €3, free under 8. 4 April to 1 November 2026.
chateau-clermont-genevois.hero_alt: Renaissance inner courtyard of the Château de Clermont with three levels of arcaded galleries inspired by the Palazzo Farnese at Caprarola, Haute-Savoie
chateau-clermont-genevois.body.what_is:
The Château de Clermont at Clermont-en-Genevois (74270) is a little-known heritage gem: a rare example of Italian Renaissance architecture in the former Duchy of Savoy. Built between 1576 and 1580 by Monsignor Gallois de Regard (1512–1582), bishop of Bagnorea in Italy and chamberlain to Pope Paul IV, it draws directly on the Palazzo Farnese at Caprarola which he knew from his time in Italy. Listed as a Monument Historique in 1950 (façades and inner courtyard), with further protection in 1988 (interiors and roofs); owned by the Haute-Savoie Departmental Council since 1949.

The inner courtyard is the marvel of the Château de Clermont: three superposed levels of arcaded galleries in the Italian manner, with carved foliate ornament, friezes and mascarons and monumental staircases. This architectural treatment is unparalleled in Haute-Savoie and Savoy, where most local castles are either reworked medieval structures or 19th-century Neo-Gothic. Archaeological work is also ongoing near the château on the 13th-century medieval castle it succeeded.

2026 prices are exceptionally affordable: adult €3, reduced €2 (students, jobseekers, Pass'Région), free under 8 plus free on the first Sunday of the month and during the European Heritage Days in mid-September, school groups €1.50. Cultural season from 4 April to 1 November 2026: weekends and school holidays outside summer, Tuesday to Sunday in July and August, closed Monday (except JEP). Heat-wave closures may apply. Partially wheelchair accessible (courtyard and ground floor only). Free parking. One of the most affordable Renaissance châteaux in France for its heritage value.

chateau-des-rubins-sallanches.name: Château des Rubins
chateau-des-rubins-sallanches.meta_title: Château des Rubins · Observatoire des Alpes · €7.50 · Sallanches
chateau-des-rubins-sallanches.meta_description: 14th-century fortified manor, renovated 2019–2021, with the 500 m² Observatoire des Alpes. 50+ interactive modules on 4 floors. Full wheelchair access. Adult €7.50.
chateau-des-rubins-sallanches.hero_alt: Medieval façade of the Château des Rubins, 14th-century fortified manor at Sallanches renovated 2019–2021 housing the Observatoire des Alpes, Haute-Savoie
chateau-des-rubins-sallanches.body.what_is:
The Château des Rubins at Sallanches (74700) — sometimes known locally as the 'Maison des Chattes Grises' — is an exceptional heritage building in the Mont-Blanc valley. A 14th-century fortified manor and former residence of the Loche family (bailiffs of Faucigny) in the 16th century, it is remarkable for having been spared by both the French Revolution and the great Sallanches fire of 1840, which destroyed almost the entire old town. It is one of the very few medieval buildings preserved at Sallanches.

From 2019 to 2021 the commune of Sallanches carried out a major heritage renovation to create the Observatoire des Alpes, an immersive interpretation centre on the mountain environment spread over 500 m² and 4 floors, with around fifty interactive modules. The thematic journey covers geology and glaciology, Alpine fauna and flora, the human history of the mountain and climate and adaptation. The route is family-friendly but scientifically rigorous, and works equally well for school groups.

2026 prices: adult €7.50, reduced €6.50 (child 5–18, student, jobseeker, senior 65+), family pass €23 (2 adults + 2 children, saving €5), free under 5 and for visitors with disabilities. Open year-round: Wed–Sun 2pm–6pm outside school holidays, daily 10am–6pm during school holidays and in July–August. Closed 25 December and 1 January. Fully wheelchair accessible thanks to the lift installed during the 2019–2021 renovation — a rarity for a historic castle. Sallanches-Combloux-Megève SNCF station is 10 minutes' walk; free parking nearby.

chateau-la-rochette-lully.name: Château de la Rochette
chateau-la-rochette-lully.meta_title: Château de la Rochette · Lully · FREE · MH 1932
chateau-la-rochette-lully.meta_description: 13th-c. castle of the lords of Cervens, cylindrical keep + round towers. Listed MH 1932. Exteriors free, privately owned. Commune of Lully.
chateau-la-rochette-lully.hero_alt: 13th-century cylindrical keep and round corner towers of the Château de la Rochette at Lully, Haute-Savoie, listed Monument Historique in 1932
chateau-la-rochette-lully.body.what_is:
The Château de la Rochette at Lully (74890) is one of the most affecting medieval remains of the Bas-Chablais. A 13th-century castle, built by the lords of Cervens (a cadet branch of the comital Faucigny family), it documents the rural Savoyard defensive system of the late Middle Ages on the plain of the Chablais between Annemasse and Thonon.

This geographical precision helps avoid the toponymic confusion frequent in the Chablais. The château is listed as a Monument Historique (1932), under Mérimée notice PA00118405, and benefits from full heritage protection.

The architecture is typical of the late 13th century: a central cylindrical keep (a military innovation over the older square towers), round corner towers and a partly preserved curtain wall. The site is in ruins, but the remains are visible from the outside of the private estate. NOTE: the château is privately owned — access from inside the property is NOT permitted without the owner's consent. Only the outer approaches (commune road and path) are freely and openly accessible year-round, via the Impasse du Château at Lully. Car recommended (no direct public transport); Bons-en-Chablais SNCF station (Léman Express) 5 km, A40 Bons-en-Chablais exit 10 minutes.

chateau-montrottier-lovagny.name: Château de Montrottier
chateau-montrottier-lovagny.meta_title: Château de Montrottier · Lovagny · €10 · Cabinet of curiosities
chateau-montrottier-lovagny.meta_description: 13th-c. fortified manor + cabinet of curiosities with 1,500 pieces (samurai, bronzes). 36 m keep with views of the Fier Gorges. Adult €10, open 29 March–31 Oct 2026.
chateau-montrottier-lovagny.hero_alt: 15th-century cylindrical keep and Neo-Gothic façade of the Château de Montrottier overlooking the landscaped park at Lovagny above the Fier Gorges
chateau-montrottier-lovagny.body.what_is:
The Château de Montrottier at Lovagny (74330) is one of the most striking castles in Haute-Savoie: a 13th-century fortified manor, remodelled in the 15th (cylindrical keep), the 18th (main residence) and finally restored in Neo-Gothic style in the 19th by Léon Marès, a wealthy collector who acquired the estate in 1906. At his death in 1916 he bequeathed the castle and its collections to the Académie Florimontane, which still manages the site. The spectacular setting overlooks the Fier Gorges just 800 metres away.

The highlight of the visit is the cabinet of curiosities, unique in the Pays de Savoie: over 1,500 pieces assembled by Léon Marès. 17th-century Japanese samurai armour, faience, antique African and Asian weapons, bronzes, manuscripts, ivories and ethnographic objects offer a journey across world civilisations. The 36-metre cylindrical keep (15th century) is climbed by a spiral staircase and gives an exceptional panorama over the Fier Gorges and the Semnoz range. A 4-hectare landscaped park with arboretum and formal gardens completes the visit.

2026 prices: adult €10, child 6–15 €6, free under 6, reduced €8 (students, jobseekers, seniors 65+), family pass €28 (2 adults + 2 children, saving €4), adult groups (15+) €8 with guided tour, school groups €4. Open from 29 March to 31 October 2026: Wed–Sun 2pm–6pm in March–May and September–October, daily 10am–6pm in July–August. Free parking, partial wheelchair access (ground floor and park accessible; keep not adapted). Car recommended (no direct public transport).

chateau-ripaille-thonon.name: Château de Ripaille
chateau-ripaille-thonon.meta_title: Château de Ripaille · Thonon · €12 · Residence of Amadeus VIII
chateau-ripaille-thonon.meta_description: 14th-c. residence of Amadeus VIII (Pope Felix V), charterhouse, 1892–1903 restoration. Arboretum + AOC Ripaille wines. Adult €12, open 1 April–2 November 2026.
chateau-ripaille-thonon.hero_alt: Château de Ripaille with its 4 towers and 14th-century fortified enclosure restored 1892–1903, on the 53-hectare lakeshore estate at Thonon
chateau-ripaille-thonon.body.what_is:
The Château de Ripaille at Thonon-les-Bains (74200) is one of the major heritage sites of the Chablais: residence of the Dukes of Savoy from the 14th century, and the retreat of Duke Amadeus VIII (later Pope Felix V), who retired there in 1434 with 5 knights of the Order of Saint Maurice. A charterhouse founded by Amadeus VIII in 1410 completes the estate. Confiscated at the Revolution and bought in 1892 by the Mulhouse industrialist Frédéric Engel-Gros (DMC textiles), it received a sweeping Neo-medieval historicist restoration between 1892 and 1903 by architect Charles Schulé. Of the original seven towers, four still rise above the 14th-century walls.

The 53-hectare estate on the shore of Lake Geneva is unique in Haute-Savoie: a 19th–20th-century arboretum (rare species, giant sequoias), a reconstructed medieval garden, AOC Ripaille vineyards (white chasselas) across 23 hectares and the adjoining 350-hectare Ripaille state forest. The Fondation Ripaille (Necker-Engel family, recognised as a public-interest charity) manages the estate and its working vineyard. The original Engel-Gros Belle-Époque furniture is preserved in the apartments.

2026 prices: adult €12, reduced €9 (students, jobseekers, seniors 65+), family pass €29 (2 adults + 2 children, saving €5), child with disability + carer €5, audioguide €3 supplement (FR/EN/DE/IT), free under 7. Open 1 April to 2 November 2026, plus Christmas window (22 Dec 2025 – 4 Jan 2026) and Zone A school holidays. April–June and Sept–Oct: 2pm–5.30pm (Wed–Sun); July–August: 11am–5.30pm daily. Star't bus line A 'Ripaille' 5 min away, Thonon Léman Express station 3 km, free parking. Partial wheelchair access. 'Flash Back!' exhibition announced for 2026. AOC Ripaille wine tasting and sales on the way out.

chateau-sires-faucigny-bonneville.name: Château des Sires de Faucigny
chateau-sires-faucigny-bonneville.meta_title: Château des Sires de Faucigny · Bonneville · Guided tour €5 · 2026
chateau-sires-faucigny-bonneville.meta_description: Castle built in 1260 by Beatrice of Faucigny: Black Tower 21 m + White Tower 15 m, prison from the 16th c. to 1934. Guided tour €5. Exteriors free year-round.
chateau-sires-faucigny-bonneville.hero_alt: Black Tower 21 m and White Tower 15 m, the surviving towers of the Château des Sires de Faucigny built around 1260 at Bonneville, Haute-Savoie
chateau-sires-faucigny-bonneville.body.what_is:
The Château des Sires de Faucigny at Bonneville (74130) — also known as the Château de Bonneville or Tour de Faucigny — is one of the major medieval castles of Haute-Savoie. Built around 1260 by Beatrice of Faucigny (daughter of Peter II of Savoy), it was the main fortress of the independent châtellenie of Faucigny in the face of Savoy. Its characteristic 'Savoyard square' plan originally featured four cylindrical corner towers, of which two survive today: the Black Tower (21 m, east) and the White Tower (15 m, west). Listed in the Inventaire des Monuments Historiques in 1987.

The castle had an eventful history: converted into the departmental prison in the 16th century, it kept that function for nearly 400 years, until 1934, when the town of Bonneville bought the monument from the State. A gradual heritage restoration has been ongoing ever since, with seasonal opening to the public via guided tours led by Faucigny Glières Tourisme.

2026 guided-tour prices: adult €5, child 10–18 €3, free under 10. Limited schedule (5–8 dates a year, generally May–September), booking required via ccfg.fr or tourisme-faucigny-glieres.fr. Exteriors free year-round (rue des Grandes Chambrettes). Central position in the heart of Bonneville, 10 minutes' walk from the SNCF station (Léman Express), A40 Bonneville exit 5 minutes. NOTE: an English-language Tourist Office mentions 'temporary closure' for restoration, but bonneville.fr and ccfg.fr confirm scheduled visits in 2025–2026 — check the exact calendar before travelling. Combinable with the Château de Beauregard and the Château de Châtillon-sur-Cluses.

col-de-la-colombiere.name: Col de la Colombière
col-de-la-colombiere.meta_title: Col de la Colombière · Le Reposoir (Haute-Savoie) | Loisirs 74
col-de-la-colombiere.meta_description: Legendary 1,613 m Tour de France pass between Le Reposoir and Le Grand-Bornand. Route des Grandes Alpes, Aravis–Bargy panorama, hiking and cycling.
col-de-la-colombiere.hero_alt: Col de la Colombière at Le Reposoir in Haute-Savoie
col-de-la-colombiere.body.what_is:
The Col de la Colombière (1,613 m) links Le Reposoir to Le Grand-Bornand via the D4, forming one of the iconic stages of the Route des Grandes Alpes. A must for cyclists, the pass has hosted the Tour de France more than 20 times since 1960 and ranks among the legendary climbs of the Northern Alps. On the Reposoir side (north), the climb is brutal, with gradients of up to 11% in the final kilometres; on the Grand-Bornand side (south), the ascent is more gradual but just as spectacular.

At the top, the panorama sweeps across the Aravis and Bargy ranges, with the Pointe Percée (2,750 m) as a backdrop. A mountain restaurant, a car park and picnic areas let walkers and cyclists make the most of the spot. Several hiking trails leave from the col: the Tête des Annes (1,869 m), the Doran refuge (1,495 m, 1h30 walk) and the Lac de Peyre (2,080 m). Ibex regularly graze the surrounding pastures, and the col is a prime spot for observing the bearded vulture.

The road is closed in winter, usually from mid-November to early May depending on snow cover. In 2026 the col reopened on 6 May at 2pm. Access is free year-round during the open season, and a free shuttle bus runs to the col from Le Grand-Bornand from 11 July to 21 August.

croisiere-bateaux-annecy-annecy.name: Compagnie des Bateaux du Lac d'Annecy
croisiere-bateaux-annecy-annecy.meta_title: Compagnie des Bateaux du Lac d'Annecy · 2026
croisiere-bateaux-annecy-annecy.meta_description: 100% electric-hybrid fleet achieved end of 2024 - first French cruise operator to reach the target. 5 boats including the Libellule restaurant boat.
croisiere-bateaux-annecy-annecy.hero_alt: Passenger cruises - 100% electric-hybrid 5-boat fleet of the Compagnie des Bateaux du Lac d'Annecy at Annecy, Haute-Savoie
croisiere-bateaux-annecy-annecy.body.what_is:
The Compagnie des Bateaux du Lac d'Annecy is the main passenger cruise operator on Lake Annecy, with a fleet of five boats: Allobroge, Belle Étoile, Cygne, La Savoie and Le Libellule (restaurant boat). At the end of 2024 the company reached its 100% electric or hybrid propulsion target — a year ahead of schedule and a first for a French cruise operator.

Boarding is at place aux Bois in Annecy. Commented cruises last 1 hour or 1 h 30 with views of the lake's mountains (La Tournette, Bauges, Bornes). Daily departures are timed to the season: in high season every hour from 10 am to 6 pm; in low season the timetable is reduced — check before travelling.

2026 fares: adult €19 (1 h) / €22 (1 h 30), child 4–12 €13.50 / €15, 0–3 €2. Open year-round. Partial accessibility for wheelchair users (the Cygne is the accessible boat).

croisiere-cgn-evian.name: CGN Évian Stop
croisiere-cgn-evian.meta_description: CGN's Évian stop on Lake Geneva: N1 Lausanne–Évian line year-round, served by the world's most prestigious Belle Époque fleet and modern vessels.
croisiere-cgn-evian.hero_alt: Lake Geneva passenger cruise — N1 Lausanne–Évian line, CGN Évian stop at Évian-les-Bains, Haute-Savoie
croisiere-cgn-evian.body.what_is:
The CGN Évian stop serves the year-round N1 Lausanne–Évian line on Lake Geneva, operated by the Compagnie Générale de Navigation sur le Léman, the historic operator founded in 1873. The crossing takes about 35 minutes. Boarding is at Quai Albert Besson in the port of Évian-les-Bains.

The CGN runs the most prestigious Belle Époque fleet in the world: five historic late-19th to early-20th-century steamers (Italie, Vevey, La Suisse, Simplon, Savoie), classified as Swiss historic monuments and still in service, complemented by modern wheelchair-accessible boats.

2026 fares: adult from €16.80 (single); free under 5, reduced fare under 16. Year-round service; high-season frequencies, reduced in winter — check cgn.ch. Modern boats are wheelchair-accessible.

croisiere-cgn-thonon.name: CGN Thonon Stop
croisiere-cgn-thonon.meta_description: CGN's Thonon stop at Port de Rives: N2 Lausanne–Thonon line by modern Naviexpress vessels year-round, plus seasonal Belle Époque calls.
croisiere-cgn-thonon.hero_alt: Lake Geneva passenger cruise — N2 Lausanne–Thonon line, CGN Thonon stop at Thonon-les-Bains, Haute-Savoie
croisiere-cgn-thonon.body.what_is:
The CGN Thonon stop runs the N2 Lausanne-Ouchy ↔ Thonon-les-Bains line year-round, with a 55-minute crossing of Lake Geneva. Boarding is at Port de Rives, in the historic heart of Thonon. Most departures are operated by the Naviexpress service, a fleet of modern fast catamarans.

In high season the stop also welcomes some of the CGN's five historic Belle Époque steamers (Italie, Vevey, La Suisse, Simplon, Savoie — all classified as Swiss historic monuments). These boats run gourmet cruises and lake tours, giving passengers a chance to sail aboard a turn-of-the-century steamer.

2026 fares: adult from €16.80; free under 5, reduced fare under 16. Year-round service; frequencies vary by season — check cgn.ch. Modern boats are wheelchair-accessible.

croisiere-cgn-yvoire.name: CGN Yvoire Stop
croisiere-cgn-yvoire.meta_description: CGN's Yvoire stop, gateway to the medieval village listed Plus Beau Village de France: seasonal N3 Nyon–Yvoire line on Belle Époque boats over Lake Geneva.
croisiere-cgn-yvoire.hero_alt: Lake Geneva off Yvoire: the Pierre d'Equarre boulder between the blue water and the Swiss shore
croisiere-cgn-yvoire.body.what_is:
The CGN Yvoire stop serves the medieval village of Yvoire, listed among the Plus Beaux Villages de France. With its 14th-century ramparts, château, Jardin des 5 Sens and cobbled lanes, Yvoire is one of the most popular destinations on the French shore of Lake Geneva.

The seasonal N3 line connects Yvoire to Nyon, in Switzerland's canton of Vaud, in just 20 minutes. The crossings are run aboard the CGN's prestigious Belle Époque fleet, giving passengers a short but memorable lake trip between Switzerland and France.

2026 fares: adult from €16.80; free under 5, reduced fare under 16. Season: weekends 4 April–29 May; daily 30 May–20 September; weekends 21 September–1 November. Wheelchair-accessible.

domaine-du-tornet.name: Domaine du Tornet
domaine-du-tornet.meta_title: Domaine du Tornet · Lake, pumptrack, playground (74)
domaine-du-tornet.meta_description: 7 ha lake, pumptrack, 2025 playground, fitness trail, jetty and Asian cherry blossom. Free access year-round at La Balme-de-Sillingy.
domaine-du-tornet.hero_alt: Domaine du Tornet at La Balme-de-Sillingy: lake and cherry trees, Haute-Savoie
domaine-du-tornet.body.what_is:
The Domaine du Tornet — locally also called the Domaine de loisirs du Tornet or simply lac de La Balme — is a public open-air leisure space wrapped around the 7-hectare lake that gives La Balme-de-Sillingy its identity. The site belongs to the commune, which has been redeveloping it in phases since 2021 after a public consultation.

This is not a campsite or a paid theme park, but a free public space. Locals come here to walk or stroll round the lake, ride the pumptrack, use the fitness trail, let the children loose on the playground inaugurated in summer 2025, or stop on the observation jetty added in late 2024.

A seasonal highlight is the national collection of Asian flowering cherries, which bursts into bloom between late March and mid-April. Free access year-round, on foot or by car (parking on site).

ecomusee-paysalp-viuz-en-sallaz.name: Écomusée Paysalp
ecomusee-paysalp-viuz-en-sallaz.meta_title: Écomusée Paysalp Viuz-en-Sallaz · Peasant Museum · 2026 fares
ecomusee-paysalp-viuz-en-sallaz.meta_description: Faucigny peasant heritage ecomuseum since 1980, ecomuseum label 2003. 8 sites, 500 m² immersive display on rural life 1850–1950. Adult €7, free under 5.
ecomusee-paysalp-viuz-en-sallaz.hero_alt: Reconstructed 19th-century Savoyard peasant interior at the Écomusée Paysalp in Viuz-en-Sallaz
ecomusee-paysalp-viuz-en-sallaz.body.what_is:
The Écomusée Paysalp is the peasant heritage ecomuseum of the Faucigny, located at 628 avenue de Savoie in Viuz-en-Sallaz. Run by the Paysalp non-profit (founded in the 1980s), it was awarded the national ecomuseum label in 2003 and now welcomes around 40,000 visitors a year.

The main Peasant Museum offers 500 m² of immersive exhibition on mountain life from 1850 to 1950: reconstructed farmhouse interiors, agricultural tools, traditional costumes and old trades. Two highlights: a fully restored 1924 classroom and 'Le Kube', an aerial film projected inside a panoramic cube.

Paysalp manages a total of eight Faucigny heritage sites, including the Prieuré de Peillonnex, the Château de Faucigny and the Verger conservatoire de Sevraz (80 heritage apple and pear varieties). Fares: adult €7, children 5–15 €5, free under 5. Note: open Monday–Friday, closed weekends and public holidays.

ecomusee-peche-et-du-lac-thonon.name: Fishing and Lake Ecomuseum
ecomusee-peche-et-du-lac-thonon.meta_title: Écomusée de la pêche et du lac Thonon · Lake Geneva aquariums · 2026
ecomusee-peche-et-du-lac-thonon.meta_description: The only place with aquariums in Haute-Savoie, at the Port de Rives. 2 Lake Geneva aquariums, fishermen's huts. Adult €3.50, free under 18 and every Sunday.
ecomusee-peche-et-du-lac-thonon.hero_alt: Traditional wooden fishermen's huts at the Port de Rives in Thonon housing the Écomusée de la pêche et du lac at the foot of the Château de Rives
ecomusee-peche-et-du-lac-thonon.body.what_is:
The Écomusée de la pêche et du lac is the only museum with public aquariums in Haute-Savoie. Run by the town of Thonon-les-Bains since 1987, it occupies three traditional fishermen's huts at the historic Port de Rives, a fishing district dating back to the 12th century.

Inside, two Lake Geneva aquariums show local species (perch, féra, Arctic char, burbot, trout, carp, roach, signal crayfish), alongside a naturalised ornithology collection, traditional fishing gear (boats, traps, nets, period and modern engines), the preserved Paul Perroud hut kept as a mid-20th-century fisherman's workshop, and a documentary on today's professional fishing on Lake Geneva.

2026 season: open from 30 May to 30 September. Very affordable fares: adult €3.50, reduced €2.60, free under 18, free for everyone every Sunday. A combined ticket with the Musée du Chablais costs €6/€4 for both municipal museums.

ereel-annecy-sillingy.name: E.réel Annecy
ereel-annecy-sillingy.meta_title: E.réel Annecy · VR centre at Sillingy · 80+ VR experiences
ereel-annecy-sillingy.meta_description: 300 m² virtual-reality centre at Grand Épagny (Sillingy): 80+ VR experiences, from age 8 with an adult. From €14 per session. Sibra bus line 7.
ereel-annecy-sillingy.hero_alt: Player immersed in VR on an individual station at the E.réel virtual-reality centre at Sillingy near Annecy
ereel-annecy-sillingy.body.what_is:
E.réel Annecy is a 300 m² virtual-reality centre located at Grand Épagny, in the commune of Sillingy (the name 'Annecy' refers to the wider Annecy basin — the venue is not in Annecy itself). The catalogue lists more than 80 VR experiences on individual 9 m² stations: action games, VR escape rooms, art experiences, sports simulators, educational and cultural content.

The centre is open to children from age 8 with an adult, and from 16 alone, with no upper age limit. It hosts families, groups of friends, children's and teen birthdays, hen and stag parties, corporate seminars and team-building events. Companions wait in a free, comfortable lounge area during sessions.

Sessions are sold in 30-minute or 1-hour slots, from €14 per person. Off-peak discounts apply outside Wednesdays, French school holidays and public holidays. The Pass culture is accepted. Open year-round — ideal for rainy days and evenings in the Annecy basin.

espace-tairraz-musee-des-cristaux-chamon.name: Espace Tairraz
espace-tairraz-musee-des-cristaux-chamon.meta_title: Espace Tairraz Chamonix · Crystal Museum · 2026 fares
espace-tairraz-musee-des-cristaux-chamon.meta_description: Renovated Crystal Museum and Mountaineering Centre in the heart of Chamonix: 1,900 Mont-Blanc mineralogy pieces. Adult €8, free under 18.
espace-tairraz-musee-des-cristaux-chamon.hero_alt: Display cases of Mont-Blanc crystals and minerals at the Espace Tairraz in central Chamonix
espace-tairraz-musee-des-cristaux-chamon.body.what_is:
The Espace Tairraz is a twin museum in the heart of Chamonix, on the Saint-Michel car-park forecourt: it combines the Crystal Museum (renovated in 2021) and the Mountaineering Centre. Run by the Communauté de Communes de la Vallée de Chamonix-Mont-Blanc (CCVCMB), it is part of the same network as the future Musée du Mont-Blanc and the Maison de la Mémoire Janny Couttet.

The Crystal Museum displays nearly 1,900 mineralogy pieces from the collections of the Musée Alpin and the Chamonix–Mont-Blanc and Northern Alps Mineralogy Club. The collection is internationally renowned, with an exceptional set of pink-red fluorites, smoky quartz, axinites, siderites and epidotes, most of them from the Mont-Blanc massif. The temporary exhibition 'Trésor minéral: the Raymond Thibault collection' runs until December 2027.

Fares: adult €8, reduced €5.50, free under 18. Guided tours on Thursdays at 2.15 pm and 4.15 pm during French school holidays, led by a crystal-hunter from the Mineralogy Club. The CCVCMB Museum Pass at €25/year covers six valley sites. Easy wheelchair access via a direct lift from the Saint-Michel car park.

filenvol-monnetier-mornex.name: Filenvol
filenvol-monnetier-mornex.meta_description: Suspended catamaran-net Parcabout 3–15 m up at the foot of the Salève. No harness, from birth. Open 365 days/year. Adult €25, seniors €16.
filenvol-monnetier-mornex.hero_alt: Catamaran nets suspended between the trees at the Filenvol Parcabout at the foot of the Petit Salève in Monnetier-Mornex
filenvol-monnetier-mornex.body.what_is:
Filenvol is a unique Haute-Savoie adventure park at the foot of the Petit Salève, in Monnetier-Mornex — 30 minutes from Annecy and 15 minutes from Annemasse. Its centrepiece is the Parcabout: a sculpture of ropes, fishing and catamaran nets suspended in the trees between 3 and 15 metres above the ground. Visitors walk, run, bounce and slide on the nets with no safety harness at all.

Three entry/exit points feed the Parcabout, with no fixed direction of travel. Children under 5 have their own safe space, the 'Toupe-Toupe'. Time on the nets is capped at three hours (the landscaped park stays open). The site is open 365 days a year, summer and winter alike.

Fares are clear: free 0–4, seniors 65+ €16, ages 5–65 €25, with group discounts (€24 from 4 people, €20 from 10) and a 10-entry pass at €200 (transferable, no expiry). Birthday packages from €115 (3 h) or €205 (3 h + snack). The Parcadrone, the site's second activity, is temporarily closed.

fonderie-paccard-sevrier.name: Fonderie Paccard
fonderie-paccard-sevrier.meta_title: Fonderie Paccard Sevrier · Bell Museum · 2026 fares
fonderie-paccard-sevrier.meta_description: Family-run bell foundry since 1796 on Lake Annecy: museum, tour of the working foundry, casting on Thursdays. Adult €9.50, free under 10.
fonderie-paccard-sevrier.hero_alt: Bronze and bells in the Fonderie Paccard workshop at Sevrier on the shore of Lake Annecy
fonderie-paccard-sevrier.body.what_is:
The Fonderie Paccard is one of the last three working bell foundries of its size in France, founded in Sevrier in 1796 by Antoine Paccard. On the western shore of Lake Annecy, the site combines a Bell Museum that traces 230 years of history (museum founded in 1984) and the working foundry, where the seventh generation of bell founders still casts bells shipped around the world.

The foundry has cast more than 120,000 bells since 1796, including some world-famous masterpieces: the Savoyarde (1891, 18,835 kg — the largest bell in France, at Sacré-Cœur in Montmartre), the 57 replicas of the American Liberty Bell (1950) and the World Peace Bell (1998, 33 tonnes — the world's largest swinging bell, Newport, Kentucky).

Several visit formats are offered: self-guided museum visit (€9.50), guided museum + bell tower (€13, daily), guided museum + foundry (€14, weekends only) and the Thursday Discovery Tour (€20) with video, guided museum visit, live bell-casting demonstration and Ars Sonora concert — except in August (no casting due to summer heat). Free under 10.

gr96-bornes-aravis-haute-savoie.name: GR® 96 Bornes-Aravis
gr96-bornes-aravis-haute-savoie.meta_description: GR® 96 Aix-les-Bains → Samoëns: 207 km total including 80 km in Haute-Savoie, crossing the Bornes, Aravis, Arve valley and Giffre.
gr96-bornes-aravis-haute-savoie.hero_alt: GR® long-distance hiking trail GR® 96 Bornes-Aravis at Saint-Pierre-en-Faucigny, Haute-Savoie — 207 km total · ~80 km in 74, moderate to difficult
gr96-bornes-aravis-haute-savoie.body.what_is:
The GR® 96 Bornes-Aravis is a long-distance hiking trail running from Aix-les-Bains (Savoie) to Samoëns (Haute-Savoie), a total of 207 km. Around 80 km lie in Haute-Savoie, crossing some of the region's most iconic ranges.

The Haute-Savoie section threads through the Bornes massif (via the Col de Bluffy), the Aravis (Saint-Jean-de-Sixt, Le Grand-Bornand), then descends towards the Arve valley and rises again into the Giffre. It is the recommended replacement for the unofficial 'Sentier des Bornes Saint-Pierre-en-Faucigny', and is the official GR through this corner of the Northern French Alps.

Free year-round access. Difficulty moderate to difficult. Length 207 km total · ~80 km in 74, full trail 6–8 days. Best season: June to October. Not wheelchair-accessible (alpine trail).

grand-parc-d-andilly.name: Grand Parc d'Andilly
grand-parc-d-andilly.meta_title: Grand Parc d'Andilly · 2026 fares and times (Andilly 74)
