grand-parc-d-andilly.meta_description: 8th largest theme park in France with 4 worlds: summer Liberté, Médiévales, Hameau du Père Noël and Grand Parc de Noël. Adult 'Malin' €15 (D-5), 'Liberté' €18.
grand-parc-d-andilly.hero_alt: Knight in armour and caparisoned steed during a joust at the Grandes Médiévales d'Andilly, Grand Parc d'Andilly
grand-parc-d-andilly.body.what_is:
The Grand Parc d'Andilly is a family theme park set in the Forêt des Moulins at Andilly, just 25 minutes from Geneva and 35 minutes from Annecy. Spread over more than 15 hectares, it has been run since 1995 by the Le Petit Pays association and is ranked the 8th largest theme park in France by the specialist press.

The site offers four seasonal themed worlds: Les Grandes Médiévales d'Andilly (May medieval festival), Grand Parc d'Andilly summer (July–August), the Hameau du Père Noël at Saint-Blaise (spring, summer and winter) and the Grand Parc de Noël (October to January), alongside daily shows (night falconry, magic, fairground rides, farm animals) and elaborate hand-carved wooden settings unique in France.

The Hameau du Père Noël, open since 1989 in neighbouring Saint-Blaise, is one of the oldest permanent Christmas attractions in Europe. A free shuttle links the two sites in high season, and 2-park combo tickets let you visit both in a single day.

grp-littoral-leman-saint-gingolph.name: GR® de Pays Littoral du Léman
grp-littoral-leman-saint-gingolph.meta_title: GR® de Pays Littoral du Léman · FREE · 2026
grp-littoral-leman-saint-gingolph.meta_description: GR® de Pays du Littoral du Léman: 50 km in four stages from Saint-Gingolph to Champanges via Meillerie and Évian, on panoramic balconies above the lake.
grp-littoral-leman-saint-gingolph.hero_alt: GR® de Pays (GRP) walking trail GR® de Pays Littoral du Léman at Saint-Gingolph, Haute-Savoie — ~50 km · 4 stages, moderate
grp-littoral-leman-saint-gingolph.body.what_is:
The GR® de Pays Littoral du Léman is a regional long-distance walking trail that covers 50 kilometres in four stages along the French shore of Lake Geneva: Saint-Gingolph → Meillerie (the historic quarry that supplied the lakeside walls of Geneva), Meillerie → Évian-les-Bains, Évian → Thonon-les-Bains, and Thonon → Champanges.

The route mostly follows the hillsides above the lake (300–900 m altitude), offering panoramic balcony views over Lake Geneva, the Vaud Alps (Diablerets, Dents du Midi) and the Jura to the west. It is the pedestrian counterpart to the cycling ViaRhôna, with a more mountainous character.

Free year-round access. Difficulty moderate. Length ~50 km · 4 stages, full trail 3–4 days. Best season: April to November. Not wheelchair-accessible (sloping trail).

grp-tour-lac-annecy-annecy.name: GR® de Pays Tour du Lac d'Annecy
grp-tour-lac-annecy-annecy.meta_title: GR® de Pays Tour du Lac d'Annecy · 80 km · 5,140 m climb
grp-tour-lac-annecy-annecy.meta_description: FFR 80 km walking loop around Lake Annecy via Semnoz, Roc des Bœufs and La Tournette. 5,140 m climb, 5–7 days. Walking counterpart to the greenway.
grp-tour-lac-annecy-annecy.hero_alt: GR® de Pays Tour du Lac d'Annecy trail with a hiker on the hillsides above the lake, La Tournette summit and Bauges massif in view, Haute-Savoie
grp-tour-lac-annecy-annecy.body.what_is:
The GR® de Pays Tour du Lac d'Annecy is the official FFRandonnée long-distance walking loop that circles Lake Annecy from the hillsides above. According to the federation, 'its 80 km route with 5,140 m of climb runs through alpine pastures, villages, hamlets, limestone summits and natural parks, taking in some legendary passes including La Tournette'. It is the walking counterpart to the lakeside Voie Verte du Lac d'Annecy greenway.

The classic start is at Le Pâquier in Annecy. Typical stages: Annecy → Le Semnoz (1,699 m) → Roc des Bœufs (1,774 m) → Doussard → refuge de Pré Vérel → La Tournette (2,351 m) → Col de Bluffy → back to Annecy. The trail uses GRP yellow-red waymarking and crosses alpine pastures, villages, limestone summits (the Pre-Alpine ridges) and the Bauges and Bornes natural parks.

Free year-round access. Best season: May to October (passes are often snow-covered outside this window). Mountain refuges €50–70/night half-board, advance booking essential in summer. For experienced hikers — stages of 5–8 hours, 800–1,300 m climb per day. Not wheelchair- or stroller-accessible.

grp-tour-pays-mont-blanc-sallanches.name: GR® de Pays Tour du Pays du Mont-Blanc
grp-tour-pays-mont-blanc-sallanches.meta_title: GR® de Pays Tour du Pays du Mont-Blanc · FREE · 2026
grp-tour-pays-mont-blanc-sallanches.meta_description: GR® de Pays Tour du Pays du Mont-Blanc: 100% Haute-Savoie walking loop around the massif on the French side — an alternative to the international Tour du Mont-Blanc.
grp-tour-pays-mont-blanc-sallanches.hero_alt: GR® de Pays (GRP) walking loop GR® de Pays Tour du Pays du Mont-Blanc at Sallanches, Haute-Savoie — ~165 km, Pays du Mont-Blanc loop, moderate to difficult
grp-tour-pays-mont-blanc-sallanches.body.what_is:
The GR® de Pays Tour du Pays du Mont-Blanc is the French reference walking loop around the Mont-Blanc massif, designed as a 100% Haute-Savoie alternative to the international Tour du Mont-Blanc. It stays on the French side, taking in the Pays du Mont-Blanc and the foothills of the massif.

The trail crosses the main communes of the Pays du Mont-Blanc: Sallanches (classic start), Passy (with the Désert de Platé in view), Servoz, Les Houches, Chamonix (Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve), Saint-Gervais, Les Contamines, Megève and Combloux — a near-360° tour of Mont Blanc as seen from France.

Free year-round access. Difficulty moderate to difficult. Length ~165 km · Pays du Mont-Blanc loop, full trail 6–10 days. Best season: mid-June to mid-September. Not wheelchair-accessible (alpine trail).

ile-de-tortuga-vetraz-monthoux.name: Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux
ile-de-tortuga-vetraz-monthoux.meta_title: Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux · 2026 fares (children's park)
ile-de-tortuga-vetraz-monthoux.meta_description: Largest indoor play park in the French Genevois for children 0–12. Entry €14.90 (>95 cm), €9.90 (<95 cm), under 12 months free, 1 adult free per paying child.
ile-de-tortuga-vetraz-monthoux.hero_alt: Multi-level play structure and pirate ship at the Île de Tortuga indoor park in Vétraz-Monthoux
ile-de-tortuga-vetraz-monthoux.body.what_is:
The Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux is the largest indoor leisure park in the French Genevois, dedicated to children aged 0 to 12 and built around a pirate theme. Located 10 minutes from Geneva and 5 minutes from Annemasse, it covers 2,500 m² indoors, soundproofed, air-conditioned in summer and heated in winter, with seating for 450 adults.

The park is split into two zones: a dedicated under-3 area with two sensory labyrinths, ball pools and foam play, and a large 3–12 zone with giant slides, obstacle courses, zip-lines, indoor tree climbing, an electric karting circuit, arcade games and the central pirate ship that gives the park its name.

The Île de Tortuga is part of the national Tortuga network (Lille, Saint-Étienne, Paris 19, Grenoble), known for its 'one adult free per paying child' pricing — families with a single child only pay for the child. The on-site Taverne de la Tortue serves home-made burgers and poke bowls; outside food is not allowed.

jardin-cimes-passy.name: Jardin des Cimes
jardin-cimes-passy.meta_description: Landscape alpine garden facing the Mont-Blanc massif — 5 themed gardens (kitchen, sensory, exotic, alpine, rockery) at Plateau d'Assy, Passy.
jardin-cimes-passy.hero_alt: Landscape alpine garden facing Mont Blanc — Jardin des Cimes at Passy, Haute-Savoie
jardin-cimes-passy.body.what_is:
The Jardin des Cimes is a contemporary landscape alpine garden at the Plateau d'Assy in Passy (74190), Haute-Savoie. It is laid out as five themed gardens: a kitchen garden, a sensory garden, an exotic garden, an alpine garden and a rockery — a planted journey through the world's mountain flora.

The setting is the garden's signature feature: an uninterrupted view of the Mont-Blanc massif (4,810 m), the Aiguilles de Chamonix and the Dômes de Miage. Few French gardens can offer this kind of high-mountain backdrop, which the contemporary landscape design plays with throughout the visit.

2026 fares: adult €7.50, free under 12. Season: 8 May to 27 September 2026, daily 10 am–6 pm. Partial wheelchair access (the garden is on a slope).

jardin-jaysinia-samoens.name: La Jaÿsinia Alpine Botanical Garden
jardin-jaysinia-samoens.meta_title: Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia · FREE · 2026
jardin-jaysinia-samoens.meta_description: National 'Jardin Remarquable' label and scientific direction by the Muséum National d'Histoire Naturelle since 1936. 2,500 alpine species. FREE entry.
jardin-jaysinia-samoens.hero_alt: Jardin Remarquable under the scientific direction of the MNHN — Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia at Samoëns, Haute-Savoie
jardin-jaysinia-samoens.body.what_is:
The Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia in Samoëns (74340) holds the French 'Jardin Remarquable' label and has been under the scientific direction of the Muséum National d'Histoire Naturelle (MNHN) since 1936 — a rare distinction for a municipal botanical garden in France.

The garden displays more than 2,500 alpine species from mountains worldwide: the Alps (France, Switzerland, Italy, Austria), the Pyrenees, the Atlas, the Caucasus, the Himalayas, the Rockies and the Andes. All species are systematically labelled, making it both a stroll and a real outdoor science exhibition.

Free entry year-round when open. Season: 1 May–15 September (10 am–7 pm), then 16 September–31 October (10 am–5 pm). Closed 15 December–15 April and in case of snow, frost, high wind or thunderstorms. Partial wheelchair access — the garden is on a slope.

karting-mk-circuit-scientrier.name: MK Circuit
karting-mk-circuit-scientrier.meta_description: MK Circuit at Scientrier: outdoor karting track approved FFSA category 1 and French karting school. Adult and child karts plus 2-stroke karts.
karting-mk-circuit-scientrier.hero_alt: Outdoor karting — FFSA category 1 approved track at MK Circuit, Scientrier, Haute-Savoie
karting-mk-circuit-scientrier.body.what_is:
The MK Circuit, at Scientrier, is an outdoor karting track approved FFSA category 1 — competition level — and home to a French karting school. The fleet includes adult karts, children's karts and 2-stroke karts, with session rentals and professional supervision suitable for families from age 7.

According to the official mk-circuit.com, the track hosts the French karting school accredited by the FFSA: introductory courses for young drivers and stages from beginner to advanced.

2026 fares: adult €28, child €19 (from age 7). Season: March to November. Opening hours: Wednesdays, weekends and school holidays 2 pm–6 pm; daily 10 am–7 pm in summer; weather-dependent. Partial accessibility (paddock access).

karting-team-bouvier-pringy.name: Team Bouvier 74 Pringy
karting-team-bouvier-pringy.meta_description: Team Bouvier 74 at Pringy (Annecy): non-profit karting and motocross club, members and supervised courses only, no commercial walk-in rentals.
karting-team-bouvier-pringy.hero_alt: Non-profit karting and motocross club Team Bouvier 74 Pringy in Annecy, Haute-Savoie
karting-team-bouvier-pringy.body.what_is:
The Team Bouvier 74, at Pringy (commune of Annecy), is a non-profit club dedicated to karting and motocross. It operates on a traditional club model: access is reserved to members and supervised courses, with no walk-in commercial hire — unlike the karting tracks fully open to the public.

The club covers both disciplines: karting on its own circuit and motocross on dedicated land. It welcomes members for training and stages, and is a niche option in the Annecy area for those who want a club-based motorsport experience rather than a one-off paying activity.

2026 membership: adult €50/year, child €30/year. Season: year-round (weather-dependent). Hours: by booking only — non-profit club with limited access. Not wheelchair-accessible (motorsport activity).

leman-forest-saint-gingolph.name: Léman Forest
leman-forest-saint-gingolph.meta_title: Léman Forest Saint-Gingolph · Franco-Swiss adventure park · 2026
leman-forest-saint-gingolph.meta_description: Franco-Swiss tree-climbing park at Saint-Gingolph: 10 courses, 140 challenges, smugglers' zip-line and outdoor laser game. From age 2, 20 min from Évian.
leman-forest-saint-gingolph.hero_alt: Franco-Swiss zip-line above the Morge torrent at the Léman Forest adventure park in Saint-Gingolph
leman-forest-saint-gingolph.body.what_is:
Léman Forest is a tree-climbing adventure park at Saint-Gingolph, right on the Franco-Swiss border, along the Morge torrent. The park straddles the France–Switzerland border — a rare feature in Europe — and features a zip-line that crosses the border, nicknamed the smugglers' zip-line.

The park has 10 courses, including 4 for children (Bambino 2–5 and Lutin 5–8, with 30 challenges each less than 1.5 m off the ground) and 6 longer courses for ages 8+, including a black course for 16+. In total over 140 challenges: monkey bridges, Nepalese bridges, Tarzan nets, dozens of zip-lines, rock crossings and tunnels.

Safety is provided by a continuous lifeline and smart carabiners that cannot be unclipped — children climb in autonomy after briefing, parents staying on the ground. The park also offers an outdoor laser game, a snack bar with a panoramic terrace over Lake Geneva and the mountains, and welcomes groups, birthdays and hen/stag parties.

leman-kid-thonon-les-bains.name: Léman Kid
leman-kid-thonon-les-bains.meta_title: Léman Kid Thonon-les-Bains · 2026 fares · Children 0–12 park
leman-kid-thonon-les-bains.meta_description: Indoor play park for 0–12 at Thonon-les-Bains: 3 age zones, mini karting. 1–3 €7, 3–12 €10, under 1 free. Closed 23–24 May (Whitsun).
leman-kid-thonon-les-bains.hero_alt: Multi-level play structures at the Léman Kid indoor children's park in Thonon-les-Bains
leman-kid-thonon-les-bains.body.what_is:
Léman Kid is an indoor play park for children aged 0 to 12, on Impasse de la Poudrière in Thonon-les-Bains. The space is organised in three age zones (0–2, 3–5, 4–12), heated and air-conditioned, with electric mini-karting, multi-level structures, motor-skills courses and private birthday rooms.

Pricing is clear and unlimited-time: free under 1 (companion €1), €7 ages 1–3, €10 ages 3–12. The Pass Family (10 entries valid 2 years — €60 under 3, €90 for 3+) includes 2 free adult companions and €1 karting tokens. Birthday packages from €18/child (2 h, 4–8 children, 2 free adults).

A savoury snack menu is served 11 am–1 pm (orders by 12.45 pm). Outside food and drinks are not allowed, except baby food, bottles (microwave available), water and fruit. Socks are required for parents and children (on sale at reception). Note: closed 23–24 May 2026 for the Whitsun weekend.

maison-de-barberine-vallorcine.name: Maison de Barberine
maison-de-barberine-vallorcine.meta_title: Maison de Barberine Vallorcine · Summer 2026 fares and times
maison-de-barberine-vallorcine.meta_description: Vallorcine museum in a 1705 house listed as a Monument Historique. Traditional alpine architecture, open July–August. Adult €5, free under 18.
maison-de-barberine-vallorcine.hero_alt: Façade of the 1705 Vallorcine house housing the Maison de Barberine, listed Monument Historique in 2023, hamlet of Barberine at Vallorcine
maison-de-barberine-vallorcine.body.what_is:
The Maison de Barberine is a Vallorcine museum housed in a traditional alpine home built in 1705 by Nicolas Ancey, shown on the 1730 Sardinian survey map and listed Monument Historique on 5 January 2023. Located in the hamlet of Barberine at Vallorcine, about 5 km from the Swiss border, it is a remarkable surviving example of pre-modern alpine housing, with its dwelling, stable and barn under one roof.

It has been run since 1987 by the Maison de Barberine Association — Musée de Vallorcine, a non-profit founded by sixteen volunteers led by Bernard and Michel Ancey, descendants of the Ancey 'des Colas' family. The museum is part of the CCVCMB Museum Pass network at €25/year, valid across six valley sites. The official framing is traditional 18th-century Vallorcine architecture and valley ethnography, not a Walser identity.

Closed at present, the museum reopens for the summer season from 1 July to 31 August 2026: Monday–Friday 2.30 pm–5.30 pm, Sunday 3 pm–5.30 pm, closed Saturday. Adult €5, reduced €3.50, free under 18. 2026 highlight: relocation of the adjacent 'regât du Crôt' (raccard) after a 2025 3D scan.

maison-de-la-memoire-janny-couttet-chamo.name: Maison de la Mémoire Janny Couttet
maison-de-la-memoire-janny-couttet-chamo.meta_title: Maison de la Mémoire Janny Couttet · Chamonix · 2026 fares
maison-de-la-memoire-janny-couttet-chamo.meta_description: Chamonix heritage centre on rue des Moulins: archives, 1924 Olympics centenary exhibition. Adult €5, free under 18. CLOSED until 30 June 2026.
maison-de-la-memoire-janny-couttet-chamo.hero_alt: Granite façade of the former Sisters' House housing the Maison de la Mémoire et du Patrimoine Janny Couttet on rue des Moulins in Chamonix
maison-de-la-memoire-janny-couttet-chamo.body.what_is:
The Maison de la Mémoire et du Patrimoine Janny Couttet is the heritage centre of Chamonix, at 90 rue des Moulins. It occupies the former Sisters' House (or Maison Baud), built in 1828 and almost entirely rebuilt after the tragic rue des Moulins fire in 2006 (only the granite outer walls survived). Acquired by the commune in 2002, opened as the Maison de la Mémoire on 1 March 2011 and later renamed 'Janny Couttet' in tribute to the Chamonix mountain guide.

Run by the Communauté de Communes de la Vallée de Chamonix-Mont-Blanc (CCVCMB), part of the same network as the future Musée du Mont-Blanc and the Espace Tairraz, it acts as a documentary resource centre open to researchers and showcases the photographic, oral and sound archives collected by the town's Mémoires service on Chamonix history and culture.

Closed between exhibitions from 9 March to 30 June 2026. Reopens in summer 2026 with the temporary exhibition 'Chamonix 1924: inventing the Winter Olympics', marking the centenary of the first Winter Games and running until November 2026. Fares: adult €5, reduced €4, free under 18. CCVCMB Museum Pass at €25/year covers six valley sites.

maison-de-la-memoire-paysalp.name: Maison de la Mémoire Paysalp
maison-de-la-memoire-paysalp.meta_title: Maison de la Mémoire Paysalp · FREE · Viuz-en-Sallaz
maison-de-la-memoire-paysalp.meta_description: Paysalp's alpine heritage media library and conservation centre at Viuz-en-Sallaz. Mémoire Alpine database, free consultation. Mon–Fri 9 am–5 pm. 800 av. de Savoie.
maison-de-la-memoire-paysalp.hero_alt: Façade of the Bâtiment des 4 rivières housing the Maison de la Mémoire Paysalp media library and conservation centre at Viuz-en-Sallaz, Haute-Savoie
maison-de-la-memoire-paysalp.body.what_is:
The Maison de la Mémoire Paysalp is a separate site from the main Paysalp Peasant Museum, located at the Bâtiment des 4 rivières, 800 avenue de Savoie in Viuz-en-Sallaz. It is the alpine heritage media library and conservation centre run by the Paysalp non-profit, aimed at researchers, genealogists, ethnologists and lovers of Savoyard culture.

Its documentary collection is exceptional: the 'Mémoire Alpine' database (memoire-alpine.com) gathers photographic, audiovisual and textual archives on Savoyard culture, agropastoralism and alpine ethnology. The Watt & Bike installation lets visitors pedal to listen to oral testimonies from farmers, craftsmen and elders. Bespoke heritage programmes can be booked through Paysalp.

Free entry for media-library use. Paid bespoke activities. Public consultation hours: Monday–Friday 9 am–12.30 pm and 1.30 pm–5 pm, Wednesday afternoon 1.30 pm–5 pm, and the first Saturday of the month 9.30 am–5 pm. Access via the rear of the MJC. A natural complement to the main Paysalp Peasant Museum 200 m away at 628 avenue de Savoie.

maison-du-lieutenant-servoz.name: Maison du Lieutenant
maison-du-lieutenant-servoz.meta_title: Maison du Lieutenant Servoz · Free entry · 2026 fares
maison-du-lieutenant-servoz.meta_description: Free interpretation centre in the oldest house in the Chamonix valley: mountain agriculture, ethnobotany, animated 3D model.
maison-du-lieutenant-servoz.hero_alt: Stone and wood façade of the Maison du Lieutenant, the oldest house in the Chamonix valley, in the hamlet of Le Mont at Servoz
maison-du-lieutenant-servoz.body.what_is:
The Maison du Lieutenant is a free interpretation centre at 2231 route du Mont, hameau du Mont in Servoz. The building is the oldest house in the Chamonix valley and is run by the Communauté de Communes de la Vallée de Chamonix-Mont-Blanc (CCVCMB), in the same network as the future Musée du Mont-Blanc and the Espace Tairraz.

Contrary to some descriptions, the Maison du Lieutenant does not cover geology or mountain rescue. Its permanent exhibition is titled 'Mountain Agriculture, Heart of the Territory' and explores alpine agropastoralism — the link between yesterday's mountain culture, today's profession and tomorrow's challenges for mountain farmers. A large animated 3D model of the territory is the centrepiece, alongside edutainment modules, multimedia displays and a collection of historic objects.

The 2025–2026 temporary exhibition 'Inspirations végétales — when nature feeds creation', by artist-designer Fanny Crochard, explores ethnobotany. Free entry to all exhibitions; paid workshops by booking. The CCVCMB Museum Pass at €25/year is on sale at the network's paying sites.

maison-du-saleve-presilly.name: Maison du Salève
maison-du-saleve-presilly.meta_title: Maison du Salève · Présilly · Interpretation centre 2026
maison-du-saleve-presilly.meta_description: Interpretation centre of the Salève massif at Présilly: geology, fauna, flora, cable-car. Adult €6, family €15, free with Geneva Pass.
maison-du-saleve-presilly.hero_alt: Façade of the former Mikerne farmhouse housing the Maison du Salève at Présilly at the foot of the massif
maison-du-saleve-presilly.body.what_is:
The Maison du Salève is the interpretation centre dedicated to the Salève massif, the emblematic mountain of the French Genevois that peaks at 1,379 m and dominates the Lake Geneva basin. It sits at the foot of the massif in Présilly, in an 18th-century farmhouse historically attached to the Chartreuse de Pomier, and is run by the Syndicat Mixte du Salève.

The permanent exhibition 'Le Salève, une montagne et des hommes' retraces the geology of the massif (limestone shaped by karst erosion), early human presence (Magdalenian remains from the Paleolithic), the historic transport links including the famous Salève cable-car, the rise of mountain sports (climbing from the 19th century, hiking, paragliding, mountain biking) and the fauna and flora of the range. Panoramic films, interactive displays and discovery trails round off the visit.

Fares are accessible: adult €6, youth 6–18 €3, accessibility €3, family €15 (2 adults + up to 4 youths), free under 6. The Geneva Pass, Pass Empreintes and Pass Léman all give free entry. Unique loyalty bonus: visit once and come back free for a year with your ticket, accompanying a paying visitor.

maison-forte-hautetour-saint-gervais.name: Maison Forte de Hautetour
maison-forte-hautetour-saint-gervais.meta_description: Restored 13th-century fortified house: 'Mont-Blanc côté Val Montjoie' display, 'Mountain rescue' winter exhibition Dec 2025–Apr 2026, Archipel Art Centre. Adult €5.
maison-forte-hautetour-saint-gervais.hero_alt: Medieval façade of the 13th-century Maison Forte de Hautetour in the heart of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains, facing the Mont-Blanc massif
maison-forte-hautetour-saint-gervais.body.what_is:
The Maison Forte de Hautetour in Saint-Gervais-les-Bains (74170) is one of the most accessible heritage sites in the Mont-Blanc valley. A 13th-century fortified house, it was the last seat of the Montjoie châtellenie (the valley running up to Les Contamines). Fallen into disuse in the 19th century, it underwent a major restoration in 2012 by the commune and reopened to the public in 2015 as a municipal cultural centre, in the heart of the village 10 minutes' walk from the Mont-Blanc Express station.

The new permanent display 'Mont-Blanc, côté Val Montjoie' (inaugurated 2025) covers the history of the Saint-Gervais guides' company, local alpinism since the 19th century, the geology of the massif and life at altitude. The winter 2025–2026 exhibition 'History of Mountain Rescue' (16 December 2025–30 April 2026) covers rescue techniques, equipment evolution and the PGHM and CRS Montagne services. The site also hosts the Pôle Archipel Art Contemporain, a municipal art centre with temporary shows, workshops and artist residencies.

Very affordable fares: adult €5, reduced €4, free under 5. Open year-round: Wed–Sun 2 pm–6 pm outside school holidays, daily 2 pm–7 pm in high season (July–August, Christmas and February holidays). Closed 25 December and 1 January. Partial wheelchair access. Free municipal car park 2 minutes away.

musee-archeologique-viuz-faverges.name: Viuz-Faverges Archaeology Museum
musee-archeologique-viuz-faverges.meta_description: Archaeology museum of the Gallo-Roman vicus Casuaria. Outstanding collections, adjacent crypt church. Adult €4, free under 5. 3-site pass €7.
musee-archeologique-viuz-faverges.hero_alt: Display cases of the permanent exhibition at the Musée Archéologique de Viuz-Faverges, showing Gallo-Roman objects and the flat copper axe from Englannaz
musee-archeologique-viuz-faverges.body.what_is:
The Musée Archéologique de Viuz-Faverges is an independent site museum at 855 route de Viuz in Faverges-Seythenex, next to the church of Saint-Jean-Baptiste de Viuz. Run since its foundation in May 1972 by the Association des Amis de Viuz-Faverges (the local history and archaeology society), it is a long-standing association-run museum with serious collections from local excavations.

It tells the story of Casuaria, the Gallo-Roman vicus of about 25 hectares and 2,500 inhabitants, comparable in size to ancient Annecy. The three rooms are dominated by the Gallo-Roman period, drawn from excavations of the sanctuary, the vicus and the Thovey villa. Highlight pieces: the flat copper axe of Englannaz (2300–2200 BC), a 189-bead amber necklace (450–400 BC), a red-clay anthropomorphic statuette (1st–2nd c. AD), a 3rd-century bronze cauldron, and votive offerings from the Gallo-Roman fana (over 6,500 m² excavated in 1980).

The adjacent church of Saint-Jean-Baptiste de Viuz is part of the visit, with its archaeological crypt: 1st-century Roman building, 6th-, 8th–9th- and c. 1000 churches, 12th-century Romanesque chancel and 1696 baroque stalls. Fares: adult €4, child 5–14 €2.50, free under 5. The '3 sites, 3 stories' pass at €7 covers the museum plus the Butterfly and Insect Museum and the Lords of Faverges Donjon.

musee-chateau-annecy.name: Musée-Château d'Annecy
musee-chateau-annecy.meta_title: Musée-Château d'Annecy: 2026 prices, hours and visit
musee-chateau-annecy.meta_description: Castle of the Counts of Geneva on a rocky spur: alpine art, UNESCO lake archaeology, Alpine Lakes Observatory. Adult 6.50-7 EUR.
musee-chateau-annecy.hero_alt: Façade and tower of the Musée-Château d'Annecy overlooking the old town and the lake from its rocky spur
musee-chateau-annecy.body.what_is:
The Château d'Annecy is the medieval and Renaissance fortress that has overlooked Annecy's old town since the 12th century. Home to the Counts of Geneva (12th-14th c.) and then the Dukes of Genevois-Nemours (15th-17th c.), it was listed as a Historic Monument in 1959 and has belonged to the Ville d'Annecy since 1953. The site combines four historic lodgings and the iconic Tour de la Reine, a 30-metre keep with walls over 4 m thick.

The castle now houses the Musée-Château d'Annecy, a regional art and history museum with 19th-century Savoyard paintings, furniture and a remarkable lake archaeology section on the prehistoric pile-dwelling sites of Lake Annecy (UNESCO World Heritage since 2011). The Regional Alpine Lakes Observatory occupies the Logis Perrière, and the Tour de la Reine offers a sweeping panorama of the lake, the old town and the Aravis range.

2026 prices: adult 6.50 EUR (Sept-June) / 7 EUR in July-August, reduced 3.50 EUR, free under 12 and on the 1st Sunday of the month from October to May. Joint ticket with the Palais de l'Île 8-9 EUR. Summer hours: daily 10.30am-6pm; winter 10am-12pm and 2pm-5pm, closed Tuesdays. Partial step-free access (Tour de la Reine not accessible).

musee-cinema-animation-annecy.name: Animated Film Museum
musee-cinema-animation-annecy.meta_title: Animated Film Museum, Annecy: opens 19 June 2026
musee-cinema-animation-annecy.meta_description: New Animated Film Museum at the Haras d'Annecy, opening 19 June 2026: 'Musées de France' collection, 450 sqm permanent exhibition, 332-seat cinema.
musee-cinema-animation-annecy.hero_alt: International City of Animated Film at the Haras d'Annecy, future Animated Film Museum by Devaux & Devaux architects
musee-cinema-animation-annecy.body.what_is:
The Animated Film Museum is Annecy's new museum dedicated to the art of animation, opening to the public on 19 June 2026 at the heart of the Cité internationale du cinéma d'animation, in the rehabilitated buildings of the Haras d'Annecy (listed Historic Monument, 2007). The opening is timed two days before the 50th Annecy International Animation Film Festival (21-27 June 2026).

Operated by CITIA (which also runs the Festival and the MIFA market), the museum draws on the scientific curatorship of the Musée-Château d'Annecy, holder of the 'Musées de France' collection of more than 8,000 works (including the Émile Cohl room). The 450 sqm permanent exhibition traces the history and techniques of animated film, from the pioneers to today's studios.

The museum also features a 650 sqm annual exhibition in the former riding hall (first edition 'Ankama, 25 years of creations', 19 June 2026-31 January 2027), a 160 sqm gallery for temporary shows ('Wildwood, LAIKA', 21 June-27 September 2026), a 332-seat cinema owned by the Haute-Savoie department, and the Grenier à Images education space. Architecture by Devaux & Devaux (DDA), scenography by Scénorama.

musee-du-batiment-ville-la-grand.name: Musée du Bâtiment
musee-du-batiment-ville-la-grand.meta_title: Musée du Bâtiment Ville-la-Grand: free, building trades
musee-du-batiment-ville-la-grand.meta_description: Free community museum of building trades: 3 floors of historic tools. Cercle des Compagnons du Bâtiment. Wed-Fri 2.30-5pm, Sun 9.30am-12.30pm.
musee-du-batiment-ville-la-grand.hero_alt: Façade of the Musée du Bâtiment in Ville-la-Grand, home to the historic tool collection of the Cercle des Compagnons du Bâtiment
musee-du-batiment-ville-la-grand.body.what_is:
The Musée du Bâtiment in Ville-la-Grand is a community museum that is entirely free, devoted to the historic trades and tools of the building industry. Run by the Cercle des Compagnons du Bâtiment association, it was founded in 2012 by Jean-Pierre Maulini, a former masonry contractor, and Raymond Bardet, then mayor of Ville-la-Grand. The building is owned by the town, securing its long-term future.

The visit unfolds over three floors of 180 sqm and presents historic tools from six building trades: stone cutting, masonry, blacksmithing, joinery, carpentry and woodwork. The volunteer companions give demonstrations and explanations, and some of the tools can be handled, making the museum a hands-on and educational stop for children and craft enthusiasts.

Admission is entirely free; donations (66% tax-deductible) are welcome. Hours: Wednesday to Friday 2.30-5pm and Sunday 9.30am-12.30pm. Groups and schools by appointment via the online form or musee.batiment74@gmail.com. Full step-free access (ramp, lift, accessible WC). Free parking at Place du Porte-Bonheur (140 spaces) nearby.

musee-du-chablais-thonon-les-bains.name: Musée du Chablais
musee-du-chablais-thonon-les-bains.meta_title: Musée du Chablais Thonon: Château de Sonnaz, 2026 prices
musee-du-chablais-thonon-les-bains.meta_description: Chablais history museum in the 1666 Château de Sonnaz in Thonon. Adult 3.50 EUR, free under 18 and every Sunday. Open mid-May to mid-November.
musee-du-chablais-thonon-les-bains.hero_alt: Façade of the 1666 Château de Sonnaz housing the Musée du Chablais in Thonon-les-Bains above Lake Geneva
musee-du-chablais-thonon-les-bains.body.what_is:
The Musée du Chablais is housed in the Château de Sonnaz, built in 1666 on the ruins of the medieval castle of Thonon, in the heart of Thonon-les-Bains facing Lake Geneva. Once home to the Gerbaix de Sonnaz family, the château has hosted, since October 2008, the Thonon-les-Bains Tourist Office on the ground floor and the museum itself in the vaulted cellars.

The permanent exhibition 'A leap in time: 7,000 years of Chablais history' traces the region from the Neolithic to the 1860 annexation to France, with over 130 objects: lake archaeology, ethnology, navigation on Lake Geneva, border smuggling, costumes, military collections and works by sculptor Marguerite Peltzer.

Very affordable pricing: adult 3.50 EUR, reduced 2.60 EUR, free for under 18s and exceptionally free for everyone every Sunday. The season runs from mid-May to mid-November. Caution: the museum is not wheelchair accessible (vaulted cellars). 2026 temporary show: 'Portrait. From painting to selfie', from 18 July 2026.

musee-du-mont-blanc-chamonix.name: Musée du Mont-Blanc
musee-du-mont-blanc-chamonix.meta_title: Musée du Mont-Blanc, Chamonix: reopens 11 July 2026
musee-du-mont-blanc-chamonix.meta_description: Former Chamonix Alpine Museum, rebranded Musée du Mont-Blanc. Largest mountain museum in France, 1,800 sqm. Reopens 11 July 2026.
musee-du-mont-blanc-chamonix.hero_alt: Façade of the 1913 Chamonix Palace housing the future Musée du Mont-Blanc on avenue Michel Croz in Chamonix
musee-du-mont-blanc-chamonix.body.what_is:
The Musée du Mont-Blanc, formerly the Chamonix Alpine Museum, reopens to the public on 11 July 2026 after 5 years of renovation and 8.6 million EUR of investment. The museum occupies the historic Chamonix Palace (1913-1914), the first of the three Belle Époque palaces of Chamonix, at 89 avenue Michel Croz.

Managed by the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc Valley Community of Communes (CCVCMB) since 2010 and a 'Musée de France' since 2003, the museum holds 17,000 catalogued items across 1,800 sqm of exhibition space, making it the largest mountain-themed museum in France. The new scenography is built around three axes: Adaptations, Exploit and Décrire.

The inaugural exhibition 'Métamorphoses du regard, les Alpes magnifiées' runs from 11 July 2026 to 30 April 2027. Prices: adult 8 EUR, reduced 5.50 EUR, free under 18. The CCVCMB Museums Pass at 25 EUR/year gives unlimited access to the 6 sites of the network.

musee-montagnard-les-houches.name: Musée Montagnard des Houches
musee-montagnard-les-houches.meta_title: Musée Montagnard des Houches: 2026 prices and hours
musee-montagnard-les-houches.meta_description: Folk arts and traditions museum of Les Houches in a 1750 house. Albert Laprade collection. Adult 5 EUR, free under 18. CCVCMB pass 25 EUR/year.
musee-montagnard-les-houches.hero_alt: Wood and stone façade of the 1750 house hosting the Musée Montagnard des Houches in the heart of the village
musee-montagnard-les-houches.body.what_is:
The Musée Montagnard des Houches is a folk arts and traditions museum housed in a 1750 heritage building in the centre of Les Houches, opposite the church. Run by the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc Valley Community of Communes (CCVCMB), it belongs to the same network as the future Musée du Mont-Blanc and the Espace Tairraz, and is included in the CCVCMB Museums Pass at 25 EUR/year.

The collection was bequeathed by architect-urbanist Albert Laprade. The visit reconstructs the traditional rooms of an alpine house from the 18th to the 20th century: pèle (living room), outa (kitchen), sarto (cellar), attic, with farming and pastoral objects from 1750-1950, costumes and everyday tools.

The temporary exhibition 'Téléphériques et Compagnie' retraces the history of the cable cars of Les Houches since the 1936 Bellevue installation. Caution: museum closed in May 2026; reopening for summer 2026. Prices: adult 5 EUR, reduced 4 EUR, free under 18.

museum-des-papillons-et-insectes-faverge.name: Muséum des Papillons et Insectes
museum-des-papillons-et-insectes-faverge.meta_title: Muséum des Papillons et Insectes Faverges: 4 EUR, 2026
museum-des-papillons-et-insectes-faverge.meta_description: 5,000+ butterflies and insects from 5 continents at Château de Faverges. The only museum of its kind in Savoie/Haute-Savoie. Adult 4 EUR, 3-site pass 8 EUR.
museum-des-papillons-et-insectes-faverge.hero_alt: Colourful display cases of exotic butterflies at the Muséum des Papillons et Insectes in the Château de Faverges, Haute-Savoie
museum-des-papillons-et-insectes-faverge.body.what_is:
The Muséum des Papillons et Insectes is housed in the Château de Faverges, at 293 chemin de la Vie Plaine in Faverges-Seythenex. Run by the Amis du Muséum association, it displays an outstanding collection of more than 5,000 butterflies and insects from five continents. It is the only museum of its kind in Savoie and Haute-Savoie, and one of only three in France.

The visit is laid out in three continental rooms: Europe-Siberia (local and northern fauna), Amazonia (morpho butterflies and tropical insects) and Africa-Asia (giant moths, goliath beetles). A live stick-insect vivarium completes the trail to delight younger visitors. Temporary exhibitions refresh the offer each season.

Very affordable prices: adult 4 EUR, child 5-14 3 EUR, free under 5. The '3 sites 3 histoires' pass at 8 EUR adult combines the Muséum with the Viuz-Faverges archaeology museum and the Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges. Summer hours 17 June-31 August: Mon-Fri 2-6.30pm, Sat 9.30am-12.30pm. Out of season, Wednesday 2-6pm by appointment. Full step-free access throughout.

palais-de-l-ile-annecy.name: Palais de l'Île
palais-de-l-ile-annecy.meta_title: Palais de l'Île d'Annecy: 2026 prices and visit
palais-de-l-ile-annecy.meta_description: Medieval prison-courthouse on an islet in the Thiou, symbol of Annecy since the 12th century. CIAP, exhibitions. Adult 4-5 EUR, joint Château ticket 8-9 EUR.
palais-de-l-ile-annecy.hero_alt: Palais de l'Île on its rocky islet in the Thiou canal, emblematic monument of Annecy's historic centre
palais-de-l-ile-annecy.body.what_is:
The Palais de l'Île is one of the most photographed monuments in France: a medieval building set on a natural rocky islet in the middle of the Thiou canal, in the heart of Annecy's old town. Built from the late 12th-early 13th century, it is mentioned as early as 1325 as the prison of the Counts of Geneva, before becoming a mint (1356-late 14th c.), a seigneurial residence and finally a courthouse at the end of the 16th century.

The Palais served as a prison until 1864 and still holds cells, a torture room and oubliettes. It was listed as a Historic Monument on 16 February 1900, one of the very first in Haute-Savoie. Since 2002 it has housed the CIAP, the City of Annecy's Heritage and Architecture Interpretation Centre, in this 'Ville d'art et d'histoire'.

The visit offers a permanent trail on the urban and architectural history of Annecy from the Middle Ages to today, with regular temporary exhibitions. The Palais belongs to the Musées d'Annecy network. A joint ticket with the Château at 8 EUR (9 EUR July-August) makes the perfect heritage combo.

parc-aventure-mont-blanc-saint-gervais.name: Mont-Blanc Parc Aventure
parc-aventure-mont-blanc-saint-gervais.meta_description: 7 treetop trails of varying levels + ziplines up to 400 m + via ferrata in the Parc Thermal of Saint-Gervais. The largest adventure park in the Mont-Blanc area.
parc-aventure-mont-blanc-saint-gervais.hero_alt: Treetop adventure park, 7 trails, 400 m ziplines, via ferrata: Mont-Blanc Parc Aventure in Saint-Gervais-les-Bains, Haute-Savoie 74
parc-aventure-mont-blanc-saint-gervais.body.what_is:
Mont-Blanc Parc Aventure is a treetop adventure park in Saint-Gervais-les-Bains, in Haute-Savoie. It offers 7 trails of varying levels, ziplines up to 400 m and a via ferrata inside the Parc Thermal of Saint-Gervais. It is the largest adventure park in the Pays du Mont-Blanc area.

The seven trails are graded by level: a children's trail (from age 3), then beginner, intermediate, sporty and expert routes. A safety briefing at the start is compulsory. Spectacular ziplines stretch up to 400 m above the thermal park, with sweeping views of the Mont-Blanc range, a unique setting for treetop adventures.

2026 prices: adult 26 EUR (Grand Parcours), under 16 21 EUR, children's trail 15 EUR. Season: April-October. Hours: April-June Sat-Sun 10am-6pm; July-August daily 9am-7pm; September-October weekends 10am-6pm. Not suitable for visitors with reduced mobility (high-ropes trails).

patinoire-jean-regis-annecy.name: Patinoire Jean Régis
patinoire-jean-regis-annecy.meta_description: Patinoire Jean-Régis in Annecy, 58x28 m indoor rink: public sessions, Friday night disco, figure skating and hockey lessons. Municipal facility.
patinoire-jean-regis-annecy.hero_alt: Indoor 58x28 m ice rink, public sessions and lessons at the Patinoire Jean Régis in Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
patinoire-jean-regis-annecy.body.what_is:
The Patinoire Jean-Régis is the public indoor ice rink of the city of Annecy, measuring 58 x 28 metres. Located rue du Stade, near the Parc des Sports, it is a flagship municipal facility. The schedule combines public sessions, the Friday-night disco and coached classes in figure skating and hockey.

The rink is a key sporting facility of the city, hosting individual sessions, school groups and clubs throughout the year. The Friday disco evening from 8pm to 11pm is a highlight for teenagers and visiting tourists, with music, lighting and animation.

2026 prices: adult 4.60 EUR + skate hire 3.40 EUR, child 3 EUR. Season: year-round. Hours: Wed, Sat and Sun 2-6pm; Friday evening 8-11pm (disco); daily during school holidays. Step-free access available.

patinoire-palais-megeve.name: Patinoire du Palais Megève
patinoire-palais-megeve.meta_description: Olympic ice rink at Le Palais in Megève, a flagship facility of the prestigious resort: hockey, galas, public sessions and lessons all winter long.
patinoire-palais-megeve.hero_alt: Olympic ice rink at the Le Palais complex, Patinoire du Palais Megève in Megève, Haute-Savoie 74
patinoire-palais-megeve.body.what_is:
The Patinoire du Palais Megève is part of the Le Palais des Sports complex in Megève, a flagship facility of this prestigious ski resort founded by the Rothschilds. An Olympic-size ice rink complemented by a swimming pool and a fitness area, it hosts public sessions, lessons, hockey matches and galas throughout the winter season.

The rink welcomes the Mont-Blanc Hockey Club for home games, as well as figure skating galas and regional championships, with a packed events calendar from December to March. Public sessions alternate with sports activities, making the rink a key family destination of the resort.

2026 prices: adult 6 EUR, child 4.50 EUR. Season: mainly December to March. Hours: winter school holidays daily 2-6pm and 8-10pm; outside holidays, weekends only. Step-free access available.

patinoire-richard-bozon-chamonix.name: Patinoire Richard Bozon
patinoire-richard-bozon-chamonix.meta_description: Richard-Bozon Sports Centre in Chamonix: 60x30 m covered Olympic rink and 26x56 m outdoor rink open in winter, at the foot of Mont-Blanc.
patinoire-richard-bozon-chamonix.hero_alt: 60x30 m covered Olympic rink and 26x56 m outdoor rink at the Patinoire Richard Bozon in Chamonix-Mont-Blanc, Haute-Savoie 74
patinoire-richard-bozon-chamonix.body.what_is:
The Patinoire Richard-Bozon is the main ice facility of the Richard-Bozon Sports Centre in Chamonix-Mont-Blanc. It comprises a covered Olympic rink of 60 x 30 metres used all year, and a 26 x 56 m outdoor rink open in winter. It is one of the reference ice facilities of the Pays du Mont-Blanc.

The covered rink is used year-round for hockey, figure skating, public sessions, lessons and training camps. The outdoor rink, opened in the winter season (typically December to March), offers a unique open-air atmosphere facing the Aiguilles de Chamonix.

2026 prices: adult 6.80 EUR, child 5 EUR. Season: year-round indoors plus winter outdoors. Hours: winter (7 Feb-8 March 2026) Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri, weekends and bank holidays 2-6pm; Wed 2-6pm and 8-10pm; reduced sessions in summer. Step-free access (changing rooms and rink).

plage-d-amphion-publier.name: Plage Municipale d'Amphion
plage-d-amphion-publier.meta_description: Blue Flag 2026 - 15,000 sqm of lawn and pebble shore. Playground, beach-volley, ping-pong, pétanque. 2 lakeside cafés and snack. Nordic sauna corner.
plage-d-amphion-publier.hero_alt: 15,000 sqm lawn and pebble shore Blue Flag beach: Plage Municipale d'Amphion in Publier on Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-d-amphion-publier.body.what_is:
The Plage Municipale d'Amphion is a 15,000 sqm lawn and pebble beach with the Blue Flag label, on the shores of Lake Geneva, in Publier, Haute-Savoie. The beach combines a tree-shaded lawn and a pebble shore with playgrounds, beach-volley, ping-pong, pétanque and two lakeside guinguettes plus a snack bar in summer.

The Plage d'Amphion holds the Blue Flag 2026 label (Teragir ranking, 18 May 2026), as one of the four kilometres of Publier's lakeshore, the longest French stretch of Lake Geneva. It also features a distinctive Coin Nordic: a Finnish sauna with direct immersion in the lake, a rare nordic experience in France.

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult free, child free. Blue Flag 2026: yes. Step-free access: partial (main paths accessible).

plage-d-angon-talloires.name: Plage d'Angon
plage-d-angon-talloires.meta_description: Shaded confidential beach: a vast lawn with large trees, set away from the bustle of Talloires centre. Exceptional view of the Château de Duingt.
plage-d-angon-talloires.hero_alt: Large shaded lawn beach with view of the Château de Duingt: Plage d'Angon in Talloires-Montmin on Lake Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-d-angon-talloires.body.what_is:
The Plage d'Angon is a vast, shaded lawn beach on Lake Annecy at Talloires-Montmin. Set at the hamlet of Angon, 2 km south of the centre of Talloires, it offers a confidential, cool atmosphere away from the busier central Talloires beach, with mature trees, beach-volley and pétanque courts.

The beach faces an exceptional panorama: the Château de Duingt on its islet (11th century) and the western shore of Lake Annecy. Its south-east orientation means afternoon sun and a golden evening light, perfect for a relaxed swim or a picnic at the end of the day.

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026 (12.30-6.30pm). 2026 summer prices: adult free (paying car park nearby), child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: partial (lawn access, no Tiralo).

plage-d-evian-centre-nautique.name: Plage du Centre Nautique d'Évian
plage-d-evian-centre-nautique.meta_title: Plage du Centre Nautique d'Évian: Blue Flag 2026
plage-d-evian-centre-nautique.meta_description: Blue Flag since 2020 (confirmed 2026). 3.5 ha of lawn, 50 m Olympic pool, 160 m slide and direct Lake Geneva access via diving boards. Historic Parc Dollfus.
plage-d-evian-centre-nautique.hero_alt: 3.5 ha beach with 50 m Olympic pool and 160 m slide: Plage du Centre Nautique d'Évian in Évian-les-Bains on Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-d-evian-centre-nautique.body.what_is:
The Plage du Centre Nautique d'Évian stretches over 3.5 hectares on the shore of Lake Geneva in Évian-les-Bains, in the historic Parc Dollfus. The complex combines a 50 m Olympic pool, a leisure pool, a paddling pool, a 160 m water slide (one of the longest in France) and direct access to the lake with diving boards.

The site has held the Blue Flag label since 2020 and has renewed it in the 2026 Teragir ranking, a sixth consecutive year. Recently refurbished, with modernised pools, diving boards, changing rooms and snack-bars, it is one of the flagship beach facilities of the French shore of Lake Geneva.

Lifeguards 2 May-21 September 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult around 5.50 EUR (2025 rate, 2026 to be confirmed), child reduced 3-10 EUR. Blue Flag 2026: yes. Step-free access (Tiralo available).

plage-de-doussard.name: Plage de Doussard
plage-de-doussard.meta_description: Sand and lawn beach at the southern tip of Lake Annecy, floating pontoon, adjoining water-sports centre. Bordered by the Bout du Lac nature reserve. Accessible.
plage-de-doussard.hero_alt: Sand and lawn beach next to the Bout du Lac nature reserve: Plage de Doussard on Lake Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-de-doussard.body.what_is:
The Plage de Doussard (nicknamed 'Douss'Plage') is a sand-and-lawn beach at the southern tip of Lake Annecy at Doussard, bordered directly by the Réserve naturelle nationale du Bout du Lac d'Annecy (115 hectares, Natura 2000). The natural delta, reedbeds and protected wildlife make for an outstanding setting that combines bathing and untouched nature.

The beach offers a wide sand-and-lawn area with a floating pontoon in summer. The Club Nautique Doussard, next door, hires out paddleboards, kayaks and sailing boats. The beach is also the western terminus of the Voie verte du Lac d'Annecy, a great cycling route along the lake.

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult free (paying car park 1 May-30 September), child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: yes (ramp, reserved space, accessible showers).

plage-de-duingt.name: Plage de Duingt
plage-de-duingt.meta_description: Lawn beach facing the Château de Duingt (Ruphy) on the west shore of Lake Annecy. Lifeguards in July-August. Water-sports club, lakeside café, summer shuttle.
plage-de-duingt.hero_alt: 120 m lawn beach facing the Château de Duingt: Plage de Duingt on Lake Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-de-duingt.body.what_is:
The Plage de Duingt is a 120 m lawn beach on the western shore of Lake Annecy, facing the iconic Château de Duingt (Ruphy), a medieval fortress set on a rocky islet, one of the most photographed views on the lake. Large shade trees, gentle slope and sandy entry make for a family-friendly setting.

The beach is officially supervised by lifeguards in July and August, with a swimming area marked out by buoys. The local water-sports club next door offers paddle, kayak and catamaran hire, and the 'La Petite Fringale' guinguette serves drinks and small bites. A free summer shuttle and the lakeside Voie verte make access easy without a car.

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult free (paying car park 15 June-15 Sept, 9am-8pm), child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: yes.

plage-de-la-brune-veyrier.name: Plage de La Brune
plage-de-la-brune-veyrier.meta_title: Plage de La Brune: Blue Flag 2026
plage-de-la-brune-veyrier.meta_description: Blue Flag since 2009 (confirmed 2026). 7,000 sqm of lawn and sandy banks with paddling pool, T-shaped pontoon, diving board, snack-restaurant. Tiralo on site.
plage-de-la-brune-veyrier.hero_alt: 7,000 sqm lawn and sandy banks Blue Flag beach: Plage de La Brune in Veyrier-du-Lac on Lake Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-de-la-brune-veyrier.body.what_is:
The Plage de La Brune is a 7,000 sqm lawn and sandy-bank beach on Lake Annecy at Veyrier-du-Lac. Equipped with a paddling pool, a T-shaped pontoon, a diving board and a snack-restaurant, it is one of the lake's flagship Blue Flag beaches and a favourite of families on the eastern shore.

The beach holds the Blue Flag label since 2009 and confirmed its renewal in the 2026 Teragir ranking, for a 17th consecutive year, a strong reference for water quality and environmental management. A Tiralo is available for swimmers with reduced mobility, and beach-volley and children's games are nearby.

Lifeguards 1 July-31 August 2026 (11am-7pm). 2026 summer prices: adult free, child free (paying car park 1 May-15 September). Blue Flag 2026: yes. Step-free access: yes (Tiralo).

plage-de-margencel-sechex.name: Plage de Margencel - Port de Séchex
plage-de-margencel-sechex.meta_title: Plage de Margencel - Port de Séchex: free, 2026
plage-de-margencel-sechex.meta_description: Natural lawn beach at the mouth of the Redon near Port de Séchex on Lake Geneva. Mature oaks, wild and quiet feel, restaurants at the port nearby. Unsupervised.
plage-de-margencel-sechex.hero_alt: Natural lawn beach at the mouth of the Redon: Plage de Margencel - Port de Séchex in Margencel on Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-de-margencel-sechex.body.what_is:
The Plage de Margencel - Port de Séchex is a natural lawn beach on the shore of Lake Geneva at Margencel, Haute-Savoie. Set on a small peninsula at the mouth of the Redon river near the Port de Séchex, it is shaded by old oaks and combines lawn with a pebble-and-gravel lakeshore.

The beach has a wild, preserved feel and is one of the quietest spots in the Bas-Chablais, far less busy than nearby Anthy, Corzent or Sciez. The restaurants of Port de Séchex are within walking distance, as is the Bowling de Margencel in the village. Bathing is free and not supervised.

Lifeguards: unsupervised swimming. 2026 summer prices: adult free (blue zone parking, 3 hours max), child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: partial (shaded zones).

plage-de-messery.name: Plage de Messery
plage-de-messery.meta_description: 400 m 'Sous les Prés' lawn beach on the Messery peninsula, facing Nyon (Switzerland). Gentle slope, shade, summer snack-bar, paddle and pedalo hire.
plage-de-messery.hero_alt: 400 m lawn beach on the peninsula facing Nyon: Plage de Messery on Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-de-messery.body.what_is:
The Plage de Messery 'Sous les Prés' is a beautiful lawn beach about 400 metres long on the Messery peninsula, facing Nyon (Switzerland, Vaud canton) on the opposite shore of Lake Geneva. A gentle slope into the water, a pebble-and-gravel bed (water shoes recommended) and shaded areas make it a family-friendly stop with wide views of the Vaud Alps.

The village of Messery has two beaches: the main 'Sous les Prés' (400 m of lawn) and the smaller, more confidential 'La Pointe'. A snack-bar opens in season at Sous les Prés, along with paddle and pedalo hire. Swimming is not supervised, so parental vigilance is recommended for children.

Lifeguards: unsupervised swimming. 2026 summer prices: adult free (paying car park 3 EUR/day, 1 June-15 Sept, with badge), child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: yes (gentle slope).

plage-de-saint-gingolph.name: Plage Municipale de Saint-Gingolph
plage-de-saint-gingolph.meta_title: Plage Municipale de Saint-Gingolph: Blue Flag 2026
plage-de-saint-gingolph.meta_description: Blue Flag 2026, a first (Teragir ranking, first year). The last French village before Switzerland, on the Franco-Swiss border. Peaceful, away from the crowds.
plage-de-saint-gingolph.hero_alt: Municipal beach on the Franco-Swiss border: Plage Municipale de Saint-Gingolph on Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-de-saint-gingolph.body.what_is:
The Plage Municipale de Saint-Gingolph sits on Lake Geneva in the unique border village of Saint-Gingolph, Haute-Savoie. Saint-Gingolph is one of a kind in France: the Franco-Swiss border literally runs through its centre, with one half in France (74500) and the other in Switzerland (Valais canton). The municipal beach is on the French side.

The beach was awarded the Blue Flag label for the first time in the 2026 Teragir ranking (announced on 18 May 2026), a major recognition for a border village often less visible than its Évian and Thonon neighbours. It offers a peaceful setting away from the busier nearby resorts.

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult free, child free. Blue Flag 2026: yes. Step-free access: partial.

plage-de-sevrier.name: Plage de Sévrier
plage-de-sevrier.meta_description: Recently renovated lawn and fine gravel beach on the west shore of Lake Annecy. 94 bike spaces, direct access to the Voie verte. Ideal for cycling families.
plage-de-sevrier.hero_alt: Recently renovated lawn and fine gravel beach: Plage de Sévrier on Lake Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-de-sevrier.body.what_is:
The Plage de Sévrier is a recently renovated lawn and fine-gravel beach on the western shore of Lake Annecy at Sévrier, Haute-Savoie. Stretching for about 200 m along the RD 1508, it offers an open view of the Château de Duingt and the eastern shore of the lake, with a gentle entry into the water that is ideal for families.

The recent renovation added 94 bike spaces, the largest covered and secured bike park of any beach on Lake Annecy. With direct access to the Voie verte du Lac d'Annecy, the beach is perfect for cycling families combining the cycle path with a swim, with no need to move the car.

Lifeguards 27 June-31 August 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult free (paying parking, ticket machine 9am-7pm), child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: partial (ramp access).

plage-de-talloires.name: Plage de Talloires
plage-de-talloires.meta_description: 250x200 m sand and lawn beach with paddling pool, diving boards, slide and integrated water-sports centre. Reopens May 2026 after lakefront works. Now free.
plage-de-talloires.hero_alt: 250x200 m sand and lawn beach with paddling pool, diving boards and slide: Plage de Talloires on Lake Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-de-talloires.body.what_is:
The Plage de Talloires is a 250x200 m sand and lawn beach on Lake Annecy at Talloires-Montmin, with a paddling pool, diving boards, a slide and an integrated water-sports centre. With 440 parking spaces, it is one of the most equipped beaches on the lake.

The lakefront was redesigned during winter 2025-2026 (municipal order no. 46/2026), with new lawn, redrawn paths and modernised facilities, jointly funded by the village and the Grand Annecy authority. Reopening is scheduled for May 2026 for the summer season.

The beach has been free since 2025 (previously 2.50 EUR per visit), a notable shift compared to the Plage de l'Impérial in Annecy, which becomes paying in 2026. Lifeguards 20 June-30 August 2026 (10am-7pm). 2026 summer prices: adult free, child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: yes (ramp, showers).

plage-de-tougues-chens.name: Plage de Tougues
plage-de-tougues-chens.meta_description: Plage de Tougues in Chens-sur-Léman, on the shore of Lake Geneva near the Geneva border: lawn and pebbles shaded by century-old trees, barbecue area, playgrounds.
plage-de-tougues-chens.hero_alt: Lawn and pebble shaded beach with barbecue area: Plage de Tougues in Chens-sur-Léman on Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-de-tougues-chens.body.what_is:
The Plage de Tougues is a shaded lawn and pebble beach on Lake Geneva at Chens-sur-Léman (and not Anthy-sur-Léman, a common confusion), near the Swiss border. Century-old trees offer rare shade right at the water's edge, in a natural setting west of Thonon, on the way to Geneva.

The beach features a designated barbecue area (a rarity on Haute-Savoie beaches), children's playgrounds and picnic tables. The Restaurant du Port de Tougues nearby serves lake fish, making the beach a popular family stop for residents of the Bas-Chablais and the Greater Geneva area.

Lifeguards: unsupervised swimming - confirmation pending in the 2026 municipal order. 2026 summer prices: adult free, child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: partial.

plage-du-lac-de-montriond.name: Plage du Lac de Montriond
plage-du-lac-de-montriond.meta_title: Plage du Lac de Montriond: free, 2026
plage-du-lac-de-montriond.meta_description: Lac de Montriond, the 3rd largest body of water in Haute-Savoie. Set between cliffs and conifers, emerald 'green lake' waters at 1,072 m altitude.
plage-du-lac-de-montriond.hero_alt: Mountain lake beach at 1,072 m, the third largest in 74: Plage du Lac de Montriond on Lac de Montriond, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-du-lac-de-montriond.body.what_is:
The Plage du Lac de Montriond sits on the shore of the Lac de Montriond at Montriond, in Haute-Savoie. The lake is the third largest body of water in the department (after Lake Geneva and Lake Annecy), covering around 30 hectares, set at 1,072 m altitude in the Vallée d'Aulps.

The lake is a natural mountain lake formed by an 18th-century landslide that dammed the Dranse de Morzine valley. Hemmed in between impressive limestone cliffs and a forest of conifers, it is famous for its emerald waters, earning it the nickname 'lac vert' (green lake), the result of calcium in suspension and a depth of up to 19 m.

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026 (11am-6pm). 2026 summer prices: adult free, child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: partial (gravel paths).

plage-imperial-annecy.name: Plage de l'Impérial
plage-imperial-annecy.meta_title: Plage de l'Impérial: paying in summer, 2026
plage-imperial-annecy.meta_description: Annecy's iconic 17,000 sqm beach between Albigny and the Pâquier. Major 2026 change: the beach becomes paying in summer (fenced, with a cap and modest fees).
plage-imperial-annecy.hero_alt: 17,000 sqm imported sand and lawn beach: Plage de l'Impérial in Annecy on Lake Annecy, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-imperial-annecy.body.what_is:
The Plage de l'Impérial is Annecy's iconic beach, stretching 17,000 sqm of imported sand and lawn on the shore of Lake Annecy between the Plage d'Albigny and the Pâquier. Bordered directly by the Voie verte du Lac d'Annecy, it is easily reached on foot, by bike or jogging from the city centre in about 5 minutes.

Major change in 2026: the beach becomes paying in the summer season, according to the city of Annecy. Deputy mayor Marie Bosson stated to France 3 Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes (April 2026) that the beach will be 'secured, fenced and therefore paying' in response to crowd-control issues, with announced 'very modest' fees in the first year (final rates to be confirmed by municipal order).

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026 (11am-7pm). 2026 summer prices: adult paying from 2026 - rate to be confirmed ('very modest' first-year fees), child rate to be confirmed. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: yes (Tiralo available).

plage-municipale-thonon.name: Plage Municipale de Thonon-les-Bains
plage-municipale-thonon.meta_title: Plage Municipale de Thonon-les-Bains: Blue Flag 2026
plage-municipale-thonon.meta_description: 3 ha beach + 50 m Olympic pool + 1.50/3/5/10 m diving boards + 100 m water slide + 3,000 sqm lake swimming area. Blue Flag 2026. Adult 5.70 EUR.
plage-municipale-thonon.hero_alt: 3 ha beach with 50 m Olympic pool, Blue Flag: Plage Municipale de Thonon-les-Bains on Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie 74
plage-municipale-thonon.body.what_is:
The Plage Municipale de Thonon-les-Bains is a 3-hectare beach on the shore of Lake Geneva at Thonon-les-Bains, along the Quai de Ripaille. The largest fully equipped municipal beach on the French shore of the lake, it features a heated 50 m Olympic pool, a heated leisure pool, a paddling pool and an integrated water-sports centre run by the town.

The site offers a rare combination of facilities: diving boards at 1.50 m, 3 m, 5 m and 10 m, a 100 m water slide and a 3,000 sqm marked lake-swimming area. It is one of the few public sites in France to pair a full swimming-pool complex with a supervised lake beach. It holds the Blue Flag 2026 label.

Lifeguards 2 May-13 September 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult 5.70 EUR (rate from 25 May 2026, verified on ville-thonon.fr), child 4 EUR (5-15), free under 5. Blue Flag 2026: yes. Step-free access: yes (ramps, accessible showers).

sentier-balcon-leman-saleve.name: Balcon du Léman Trail
sentier-balcon-leman-saleve.meta_description: Ridge trail along the southern Salève between La Croisette and Cruseilles - 15 km of red-and-white waymarking, panoramic views over Lake Geneva, the Jura and Mont Blanc.
sentier-balcon-leman-saleve.hero_alt: Waymarked GR ridge trail Balcon du Léman at Cruseilles, Haute-Savoie - around 15 km Croisette-Cruseilles, moderate to difficult
sentier-balcon-leman-saleve.body.what_is:
The Sentier du Balcon du Léman follows the crest of the southern Salève - the iconic mountain of the Greater Geneva area - between La Croisette (Annemasse side) and Cruseilles. Over 15 km of red-and-white GR waymarking, the path runs between 900 and 1,380 m altitude and offers a 360-degree panorama over Lake Geneva, the Jura and the Mont Blanc range.

The trail is one of the few in Haute-Savoie to take in four horizons at once: Lake Geneva and the Vaud Alps to the north, Mont Blanc and the Aravis to the east, the Jura and the Pays de Gex to the west, and the Rhône valley with the Bugey Prealps to the south. The northern terminus links into the Salève cable car for easy connections from the Geneva side.

Free access all year, no ticketing. Difficulty: moderate to difficult. Length: ~15 km Croisette-Cruseilles, 4-5 h walking time. Best season: April to November. Not suitable for reduced-mobility visitors (ridge trail). Free parking at the trailhead; refuge nights along the way priced 50-90 EUR half-board.

sentier-bout-du-lac-doussard.name: Bout du Lac d'Annecy Discovery Trail
sentier-bout-du-lac-doussard.meta_title: Bout du Lac d'Annecy Discovery Trail - FREE - 2026
sentier-bout-du-lac-doussard.meta_description: Discovery trail at Doussard through the National Nature Reserve at the south end of Lake Annecy: 1.6 km wheelchair-accessible loop and a longer 7 km loop.
sentier-bout-du-lac-doussard.hero_alt: Interpretation trail - wheelchair-accessible since 2010 - Bout du Lac d'Annecy at Doussard, Haute-Savoie - 1.6 km accessible loop, 7 km full loop, very easy
sentier-bout-du-lac-doussard.body.what_is:
The Sentier pédagogique du Bout du Lac d'Annecy crosses the National Nature Reserve of the Bout du Lac d'Annecy, created in 1974 over 84 hectares at the delta of the Ire and Eau-Morte rivers. It is one of the two national nature reserves on the lake (alongside Sixt-Passy), a protected wetland of regional importance.

The site was redeveloped in May 2010 with a 1.6 km loop fully accessible for visitors with reduced mobility, thanks to wooden boardwalks over the marshy zones. A longer 7 km loop extends the discovery through the reedbeds, the delta and the surrounding woodland, with interpretation panels on the flora, fauna and lake ecology.

Free entry all year. Difficulty: very easy. Distance: 1.6 km accessible loop / 7 km full loop. Best season: April to October. Free parking at the trailhead in Doussard. Dogs on leads accepted, pushchairs and wheelchairs welcome on the short loop.

sentier-cascades-sixt-fer-a-cheval.name: Cascades de Sixt Trail
sentier-cascades-sixt-fer-a-cheval.meta_description: Trail through the Cirque du Fer-à-Cheval to the Bout du Monde at Sixt-Fer-à-Cheval: 6 km loop through up to 30 waterfalls inside the Sixt-Passy National Nature Reserve.
sentier-cascades-sixt-fer-a-cheval.hero_alt: Hiking trail in the National Nature Reserve - Sentier des Cascades at Sixt-Fer-à-Cheval, Haute-Savoie - 6 km loop, 200 m elevation gain, moderate
sentier-cascades-sixt-fer-a-cheval.body.what_is:
The Sentier des Cascades de Sixt runs along the floor of the Cirque du Fer-à-Cheval, one of the largest natural amphitheatres in Europe, in the heart of the Sixt-Passy National Nature Reserve (created in 1977, 9,200 hectares). The 6 km loop leads to the spot known as the Bout du Monde, at the very back of the cirque.

Depending on the season, up to 30 waterfalls can be seen along the route, including the Cascade de la Pleureuse and the Cascade de la Sauffaz. They are most spectacular during the snowmelt in May and June. The trail is easy underfoot, with around 200 m of elevation gain on a moderate gradient.

Free access all year, no ticketing. Difficulty: moderate. Distance: 6 km loop, 2-3 h walking time. Best season: May to October. Free parking at the entrance to the cirque. Not suitable for wheelchairs (mountain trail); dogs on leads accepted.

sentier-decouverte-plateau-glieres-thore.name: Plateau des Glières Discovery Trail
sentier-decouverte-plateau-glieres-thore.meta_title: Plateau des Glières Discovery Trail - FREE - 2026
sentier-decouverte-plateau-glieres-thore.meta_description: Interpretation trail on the Plateau des Glières, a major site of the French Resistance: 11 panels and the national monument by Gilioli (1973) at Thorens-Glières.
sentier-decouverte-plateau-glieres-thore.hero_alt: Historical interpretation trail - Plateau des Glières Discovery Trail at Thorens-Glières, Haute-Savoie - 4 km loop, 11 panels, very easy
sentier-decouverte-plateau-glieres-thore.body.what_is:
The Sentier de découverte du Plateau des Glières is an interpretation trail dedicated to one of the most important sites of the French Resistance. In March 1944, the plateau was the scene of the stand of 465 maquisards from the Maquis des Glières, who faced Vichy forces and then the German army. 105 fighters were killed in action or executed; the plateau has been a national symbol ever since.

Eleven information panels run along the route, set in the alpine pastures at around 1,400 m altitude, and lead to the national monument designed by sculptor Émile Gilioli, inaugurated in 1973. The plateau also hosts the annual 'Glières Citizenship Days' and a yearly torchlight tribute in March.

Free entry all year. Difficulty: very easy. Distance: 4 km loop, about 1 h 30 walking time. Best season: May to October (the plateau is snow-bound in winter and turned over to Nordic skiing). Free parking at the visitor centre, with information point and seasonal exhibition.

sentier-desert-de-plate-passy.name: Désert de Platé Trail
sentier-desert-de-plate-passy.meta_description: Désert de Platé Trail at Passy: 6.5 km return and 870 m elevation gain from Praz-Coutant to the Platé refuge, across Europe's largest karst pavement (1,980 ha, listed since 1998).
sentier-desert-de-plate-passy.hero_alt: Alpine hiking trail - Désert de Platé at Passy, Haute-Savoie - 6.5 km return, 870 m elevation gain, difficult
sentier-desert-de-plate-passy.body.what_is:
The Sentier du Désert de Platé, at Passy, leads to what is considered the largest karst pavement (lapiaz) in Europe. The Platé site covers 1,980 hectares of remarkable limestone formations and has been a classified natural site since 1998. The plateau spreads out at over 2,000 m altitude, an open lunar landscape framed by the Mont-Blanc range.

The route climbs from Praz-Coutant to the refuge de Platé at 2,032 m, then continues to the Col de la Portette, covering 6.5 km return and 870 m of elevation gain. The Club Alpin Français refuge is open from June to September and can be used for an overnight stay before exploring the plateau the following day.

Free access all year, refuge stays paid (50-65 EUR half-board). Difficulty: difficult. Distance: 6.5 km return, 4-5 h walking time. Best season: June to October. Not suitable for wheelchairs (high-mountain trail); dogs on leads tolerated.

sentier-espagnols-pas-du-roc-glieres.name: Sentier des Espagnols / Pas du Roc
sentier-espagnols-pas-du-roc-glieres.meta_title: Sentier des Espagnols / Pas du Roc - FREE - 2026
sentier-espagnols-pas-du-roc-glieres.meta_description: Historical trail on the Plateau des Glières via the Pas du Roc: cliff steps cut in 1830 by Spanish republican refugees, then a Maquis route in 1944.
sentier-espagnols-pas-du-roc-glieres.hero_alt: Historical trail - Plateau des Glières - Sentier des Espagnols / Pas du Roc at Thorens-Glières, Haute-Savoie - 8 km loop, 600 m elevation gain, moderate
sentier-espagnols-pas-du-roc-glieres.body.what_is:
The Sentier des Espagnols / Pas du Roc is a historical trail on the Plateau des Glières with a remarkable double memory. The cliff steps along the route were cut in 1830 by Spanish republican refugees who had fled to Haute-Savoie after the collapse of the Spanish liberal revolution of 1820-1823, a moving chapter of 19th-century European political migration.

The trail leads to the Pas du Roc at 1,800 m altitude, a strategic passage cut into the cliff face of the plateau. The same path then became a key crossing point for the Maquis des Glières in 1944, used by Resistance fighters to access and resupply the plateau. The route is therefore both a memorial to political refuge and to the French Resistance.

Free entry all year. Difficulty: moderate. Distance: 8 km loop, 600 m elevation gain, 3-4 h walking time. Best season: May to October. Free parking at the Plateau des Glières car park. Not suitable for wheelchairs (mountain trail).

sentier-maison-saleve-pomier-presilly.name: Maison du Salève Trails
sentier-maison-saleve-pomier-presilly.meta_description: Network of interpretation trails around the Maison du Salève at Pomier-Présilly: Petites Croix, Convers and Orjobet circuits plus the Chartreuse de Pomier.
sentier-maison-saleve-pomier-presilly.hero_alt: Interpretation trail network - Sentiers de la Maison du Salève at Présilly, Haute-Savoie - circuits 2-8 km, easy to moderate
sentier-maison-saleve-pomier-presilly.body.what_is:
The Sentiers de la Maison du Salève form a network of themed interpretation trails starting from the Maison du Salève visitor centre at Pomier-Présilly (74160), on the southern flank of the Salève. The site is just 30 minutes from Geneva and is the natural balcony of the Greater Geneva area for both Swiss and French residents.

Several circuits are signposted: the Petites Croix trail (rural heritage), the Convers trail (botany), the Orjobet trail (panoramic views) and a circuit linking the Chartreuse de Pomier, a medieval monastic site nearby. Lengths vary from 2 to 8 km, with easy to moderate difficulty - perfect for families or for an outing combined with a Maison du Salève visit.

Free access all year. Difficulty: easy to moderate. Distance: circuits of 2-8 km. Best season: April to November. Free parking at the Maison du Salève. Dogs on leads accepted; the longer circuits are not wheelchair-accessible.

sentier-roselieres-saint-jorioz.name: Sentier des Roselières
sentier-roselieres-saint-jorioz.meta_description: Natura 2000 interpretation trail through the reedbeds of Lake Annecy at Saint-Jorioz - 4.2 km return, fully accessible for wheelchairs and pushchairs.
sentier-roselieres-saint-jorioz.hero_alt: Interpretation trail - wheelchair / pushchair accessible - Sentier des Roselières at Saint-Jorioz, Haute-Savoie - 4.2 km return, very easy
sentier-roselieres-saint-jorioz.body.what_is:
The Sentier des Roselières at Saint-Jorioz is an interpretation trail that crosses a Natura 2000 protected area of lakeside reedbeds on the western shore of Lake Annecy. The site is a natural habitat of European interest, home to European beavers, water birds, dragonflies and aquatic plants - a rare experience of unspoilt wetland within easy reach of a major resort.

The trail is 4.2 km return, fully developed for visitors with reduced mobility and pushchairs: wooden boardwalks span the marshy sections and the path is flat throughout, with no steps. This level of accessibility within a protected nature site is exceptional in Haute-Savoie. Information panels along the way explain the lakeside ecosystem.

Free entry all year. Difficulty: very easy. Distance: 4.2 km return, 1 h 30 walking time. Best season: April to October. Free parking at the trailhead at Saint-Jorioz beach. Dogs on leads accepted.

sentier-tournette-montmin.name: Tournette Trail from Montmin
sentier-tournette-montmin.meta_description: Tournette ascent from Montmin: summit at 2,351 m from the Chalet de l'Aulp, 12 km return and 1,200 m of elevation gain with cabled passages.
sentier-tournette-montmin.hero_alt: Alpine trail - classic ascent - Tournette from Montmin at Talloires-Montmin, Haute-Savoie - 12 km return, 1,200 m elevation gain, difficult (cabled sections)
sentier-tournette-montmin.body.what_is:
The Sentier de la Tournette depuis Montmin leads to the summit of the Tournette at 2,351 m - the emblematic peak above Lake Annecy and the highest summit of the Bornes-Aravis range. From the Chalet de l'Aulp at Montmin, the alpine route covers 12 km return for 1,200 m of elevation gain, with a few cabled passages in the final section.

The last 200 m to the summit include fixed chains across exposed rock - manageable for a confident hiker with a head for heights but not suitable for young children. The reward is a sweeping 360-degree panorama over Lake Annecy, the Mont Blanc range, the Bauges and the Aravis from one of the most iconic viewpoints of the region.

Free entry all year. Difficulty: difficult (cabled sections). Distance: 12 km return, 1,200 m elevation gain, 6-7 h walking time. Best season: June to October. Paid parking at the Chalet de l'Aulp in season. Not suitable for wheelchairs or pushchairs.

telecabine-du-jaillet.body.what_is:
The Jaillet gondola links the village of Megève (~1,113 m) to the Jaillet plateau at 1,585 m altitude in just a few minutes. Operated by SEM du Jaillet / Les Portes du Mont-Blanc, it is the gateway in winter to the Portes du Mont-Blanc ski area (Megève, Combloux, Cordon, La Giettaz). The lower station is at 821 route du Jaillet, with free 7-hour parking.

In summer, the top of the lift opens onto a free panoramic play area facing Mont Blanc, with trampolines, climbing structures and swings. Family trails leave directly from the top station: the Lac du Jaillet loop (20 minutes), the Col du Jaillet with its Roman granite milestone discovered in 1964, and a route through the woods to Combloux. Mountain-bike trails and the Giant Swing round out the summer offer.

2026 prices: adult return around EUR 18-22, child EUR 12-15. Season: mid-December to mid-April (winter) and mid-June to early September (summer). Hours: 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. daily in season. Accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (top station and summit play area).

telecabine-panoramic-mont-blanc.name: Panoramic Mont-Blanc Cable Car
telecabine-panoramic-mont-blanc.meta_title: Panoramic Mont-Blanc Cable Car - Aiguille du Midi to Italy
telecabine-panoramic-mont-blanc.meta_description: Cross-border gondola from the summit of the Aiguille du Midi (3,777 m) to Pointe Helbronner (3,466 m) above the Géant Glacier. Summer season 4 July - 27 September 2026.
telecabine-panoramic-mont-blanc.hero_alt: Panoramic Mont-Blanc gondola crossing the Géant Glacier between the Aiguille du Midi and Pointe Helbronner
telecabine-panoramic-mont-blanc.body.what_is:
The Panoramic Mont-Blanc gondola is a cross-border cable car between France and Italy, linking the summit of the Aiguille du Midi cable car (3,777 m) to the Pointe Helbronner (3,466 m, Italy) in about 30 minutes. Operated by the Compagnie du Mont-Blanc on the French side and connected to the Skyway Monte Bianco on the Italian side, it spans 5 km above the Géant Glacier.

The journey is made in pulsed 4-seat cabins, with no contact with other passengers. The view takes in the Géant Glacier, the Mont Blanc du Tacul, the Grandes Jorasses and the Dent du Géant - one of the most spectacular glacier panoramas in Europe accessible without crampons.

At Pointe Helbronner on the Italian side, a panoramic terrace projects out at 3,466 m, with exhibitions on the minerals of the massif and a connection to the Skyway Monte Bianco down to Courmayeur in the Aosta Valley.
