Summer season: 4 July to 27 September 2026, weather permitting. Booking strongly recommended; pass priced as a supplement to the Aiguille du Midi ticket.

telepherique-aiguille-du-midi.name: Aiguille du Midi Cable Car
telepherique-aiguille-du-midi.meta_title: Aiguille du Midi Cable Car - Prices & Times 2026
telepherique-aiguille-du-midi.meta_description: Panoramic cable car from Chamonix to the summit at 3,842 m. Adult return EUR 60.20 - 83, open 7:10 a.m. to 6 p.m., Step into the Void and summit terrace included.
telepherique-aiguille-du-midi.hero_alt: Aiguille du Midi cable car climbing towards the 3,842 m summit facing the Mont-Blanc range from Chamonix
telepherique-aiguille-du-midi.body.what_is:
The Aiguille du Midi cable car links Chamonix-Mont-Blanc (1,035 m) to the summit of the Aiguille du Midi at 3,842 m in two sections and roughly 20 minutes. Operated by the Compagnie du Mont-Blanc, it is one of the highest cable cars in Europe and the quickest panoramic vantage point facing the Mont Blanc range.

At the top, visitors can step into the famous Step into the Void glass box, suspended 1,035 m above the valley; ride the summit lift to the 360-degree panoramic terrace; explore the Espace Vertical mountaineering museum at 3,777 m; watch the Mont-Blanc 4D film at the lower station; and cross to the Italian side aboard the Panoramic Mont-Blanc gondola to Pointe Helbronner (summer only).

2026 prices: adult return EUR 60.20 - 83, child rate available. Season: year-round (subject to weather and maintenance windows). Hours: from 7:10 a.m. to 6 p.m., variable depending on season. Partially accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (top section by lift). The Aiguille du Midi is also the launch point for the Vallée Blanche, the legendary 22 km off-piste glacier descent.

telepherique-du-saleve.name: Salève Cable Car
telepherique-du-saleve.meta_description: Cable car from Étrembières (432 m) to the summit of the Salève (1,097 m) facing Geneva. Adult return EUR 18, open 9 a.m. to 10:45 p.m. Thu-Sat. Free exhibition.
telepherique-du-saleve.hero_alt: Salève cable car cabin ascending facing Geneva, Lake Geneva and the Mont-Blanc range
telepherique-du-saleve.body.what_is:
The Salève cable car is a high-capacity panoramic lift linking the French commune of Étrembières (Pas-de-l'Échelle, 432 m) to the summit of the Salève (1,097 m), known as the 'Balcony of Geneva', in just 5 minutes. The two 60-seat cabins make it one of the highest-capacity lines in the region.

Co-operated by RATP Dev, TPG (Transports Publics Genevois) and COMAG (Poma), the cable car is integrated into the Geneva public transport network: TPG line 8 reaches Veyrier-Douane (a short walk to the lift), with a direct shuttle to the base on summer Sundays. A reduced fare of EUR 12 return applies on presentation of an Oùra travel card, a SwissPass GA, a Swiss half-fare card or a Geneva City Pass.

At the top, the free Salève Secret exhibition (over 100 m2), a climbing wall, the panoramic Restaurant 360, waymarked hiking trails, paragliding launches and views over Geneva, Lake Geneva, the Jura and Mont Blanc complete the offer. 2026 prices: adult return EUR 18. Hours: 9 a.m. to 10:45 p.m. Thursday to Saturday in summer; reduced opening the rest of the week.

tour-bellecombe-reignier.name: Tour de Bellecombe
tour-bellecombe-reignier.meta_title: Tour de Bellecombe - Reignier-Ésery - FREE - 25 m keep
tour-bellecombe-reignier.meta_description: Square 12th-13th-century keep, 25 m high, last remnant of a castle that controlled a toll on the Arve. Free access all year, 5 min from the car park.
tour-bellecombe-reignier.hero_alt: Square 12th-13th-century keep, 25 m high, of the Tour de Bellecombe at Reignier-Ésery, remnant of a medieval toll castle on the Arve, Haute-Savoie
tour-bellecombe-reignier.body.what_is:
The Tour de Bellecombe at Reignier-Ésery (74930) is one of the most impressive medieval remnants of the Savoyard Genevois. A square 12th-13th-century keep, it is the last surviving element of a now-vanished medieval castle - only the imposing keep withstood destruction and the passage of time.

The castle had a remarkable historical function: it controlled a toll on the Arve river, where merchants crossing between the Duchy of Savoy and the Republic of Geneva had to pay a duty levied from the fortress. That financial role justified the construction of such a powerful tower, today 25 m high (originally around 30 m) and clearly visible from a great distance.

Free access all year, no ticketing. A 5-minute footpath leads from the free car park to the foot of the keep. The site is unguarded, with no interior visit (the tower is closed to the public) but a remarkable exterior viewpoint over the Arve valley. Dogs on leads tolerated; not wheelchair-accessible (uneven path).

tour-des-langues-thonon.name: Tour des Langues
tour-des-langues-thonon.meta_title: Tour des Langues - Thonon - FREE - Port de Rives - 14th c.
tour-des-langues-thonon.meta_description: 14th-century medieval tower at the Port de Rives, last remnant of the 1283 mestralie. The name comes from a feudal due of ox tongues, abolished in 1773. Free access.
tour-des-langues-thonon.hero_alt: 14th-century medieval tower known as Tour des Langues at the Port de Rives in Thonon-les-Bains on the shore of Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie
tour-des-langues-thonon.body.what_is:
The Tour des Langues at Thonon-les-Bains (74200) is one of the last medieval remnants of the Port de Rives, the historic harbour of Thonon on Lake Geneva. A 14th-century tower, it is all that survives of a fortified house belonging to the mestralie of Thonon - the fiscal and territorial administration established by Philip I of Savoy in 1283 to collect the feudal dues of the Chablais.

The origin of the name 'des Langues' (of the tongues) is one of the most picturesque toponymic anecdotes in Haute-Savoie. The butchers of Thonon were required to deliver to the mestralie every year the tongues of every ox they slaughtered in town. This feudal due in kind remained in force until it was abolished in 1773.

Free access all year, no ticketing. The tower is visible from the lakeshore promenade and from the Port de Rives, a 5-minute walk from the city centre. Information panel on site. Not open to the public inside; viewing from outside only. Free harbour parking; the surrounding promenade is fully wheelchair-accessible.

tour-du-mont-blanc-les-houches.name: Tour du Mont-Blanc
tour-du-mont-blanc-les-houches.meta_title: Tour du Mont-Blanc - TMB - 170 km - GR® - 2026
tour-du-mont-blanc-les-houches.meta_description: 170 km in 11 anti-clockwise stages (215 km with GR® variants) across France, Italy and Switzerland. 10,000 m elevation gain. Start at Les Houches. Mid-June to mid-September.
tour-du-mont-blanc-les-houches.hero_alt: Tour du Mont-Blanc TMB trail facing the Mont-Blanc range and the Aiguille Verte with hikers and red-and-white GR waymarking, Haute-Savoie
tour-du-mont-blanc-les-houches.body.what_is:
The Tour du Mont-Blanc (TMB) is one of the world's most iconic multi-day treks: a loop around the Mont Blanc range crossing three countries (France, Italy, Switzerland), with free passage between Schengen-area borders. The classic itinerary covers 170 km in 11 anti-clockwise stages; the full GR® TMB loop with all variants reaches 215 km, with around 10,000 m of cumulative elevation gain - a serious challenge for experienced hikers.

The TMB is traditionally walked anti-clockwise from Les Houches (74310). Mountain stages cross the Col du Bonhomme, the Col de la Seigne (into Italy), the Grand Col Ferret (into Switzerland) and the Col de Balme (back into France). Stage lengths vary from 12 to 22 km, with refuges and gîtes spread along the route - booking from January onwards is essential in high season.

Free access to the trail, no permit required. Difficulty: difficult (sustained gradient and altitude). Best season: mid-June to mid-September. Accommodation in refuges priced 55-90 EUR half-board. Carry a hut booklet and check border-pass conditions. Not suitable for reduced-mobility visitors; dogs are heavily restricted in Italian and Swiss reserves.

train-du-montenvers-mer-de-glace.name: Montenvers Train - Mer de Glace
train-du-montenvers-mer-de-glace.meta_title: Montenvers Train - Mer de Glace - Prices & Times 2026
train-du-montenvers-mer-de-glace.meta_description: Rack railway from Chamonix to the Mer de Glace (1,913 m). Adult return EUR 31.50, Voyage pack EUR 49.70. Ice cave and Glaciorium included.
train-du-montenvers-mer-de-glace.hero_alt: Red Montenvers rack railway train at Chamonix station facing the Mont-Blanc range, gateway to the Mer de Glace
train-du-montenvers-mer-de-glace.body.what_is:
The Montenvers train is a rack railway brought into service in 1909, linking Chamonix-Mont-Blanc station (1,042 m) to the Montenvers site (1,913 m) in about 20 minutes. Operated by the Compagnie du Mont-Blanc, it delivers visitors to the iconic belvedere facing the Mer de Glace, the largest glacier in France (7 km long).

At the top, the site includes the gondola down to the surface of the glacier (closed for maintenance until 29 May 2026), the ice cave carved each year inside the glacier (closed until 5 June 2026), the Glaciorium interpretation centre on glaciers, the Refuge-Hôtel du Montenvers and the Grand Hôtel du Montenvers, a historic 1880 building listed as a historic monument.

2026 prices: adult return EUR 31.50, Voyage pack EUR 49.70 (train + gondola + ice cave + Glaciorium). Season: year-round, daily in summer. Hours: 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m., variable by season. The Montenvers is also a stage on the Balcon Nord, the famous high trail from the Plan de l'Aiguille (Aiguille du Midi cable car) to the Montenvers.

tropicaland-viry.name: Tropicaland
tropicaland-viry.meta_description: Dinosaur park at Viry: 35 life-size sculptures, botanical garden and mini-farm on 1.5 ha. Adult EUR 10, child 3-11 EUR 7, under 3 free.
tropicaland-viry.hero_alt: Life-size sculpture of a tyrannosaur in the dense jungle of the Tropicaland dinosaur park at Viry
tropicaland-viry.body.what_is:
Tropicaland is the only dinosaur park in Haute-Savoie, set between Geneva and Annecy a few kilometres from Saint-Julien-en-Genevois and Viry. On 1.5 hectares of dense jungle and green meadows, the park presents 35 life-size sculptures of dinosaurs and prehistoric creatures: tyrannosaurs, velociraptors, stegosaurs, triceratops, dimetrodons, spinosaurs, pterosaurs and other Jurassic giants, from baby dinos to the most imposing adults.

The park celebrates its 20th anniversary in 2026. It combines three themes: botany, with a jungle of original plants and trees; prehistoric animals (the 35 dinosaurs); and a mini-farm with domestic animals and exotic birds for the youngest visitors. A 'Dino Hunter' trail game invites children to spot the dinosaurs hidden in the vegetation and identify which are herbivores or carnivores.

2026 prices: adult EUR 10, child 3-11 EUR 7, under 3 free. Season: April to early November. Hours: 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. daily in season. Tropicaland is a family venue and does not host school groups or holiday-club outings - individual families only.

veloroute-vallee-arve-cluses-sallanches.name: Arve Valley Cycle Route
veloroute-vallee-arve-cluses-sallanches.meta_title: Arve Valley Cycle Route - 40 km - FREE - V61
veloroute-vallee-arve-cluses-sallanches.meta_description: 40 km of greenway from Bonneville to Sallanches via Cluses on the V61 Lake Geneva-Mont-Blanc route. Arve footbridge opened December 2019, smooth asphalt, flat.
veloroute-vallee-arve-cluses-sallanches.hero_alt: Arve Valley Cycle Route V61 Lake Geneva-Mont-Blanc greenway crossing the industrial valley facing the Mont-Blanc range, Haute-Savoie
veloroute-vallee-arve-cluses-sallanches.body.what_is:
The Véloroute Vallée de l'Arve is the Haute-Savoie section of the V61 'Léman au Mont-Blanc' cycle route, running through the industrial Arve valley. 40 km of greenway from Bonneville to Sallanches via Cluses and Magland, mainly on a dedicated right-of-way (around 80% of the route), on smooth asphalt 3.00-3.50 m wide. The cumulative elevation gain is moderate (around 150 m over 40 km), with mostly flat sections and a gentle climb towards Mont Blanc.

The route was developed in two phases: the Bonneville-Cluses 16 km section opened in 2010, the first major segment; and the Cluses-Sallanches 16 km section opened in December 2019, including the emblematic Arve footbridge, which allows cyclists to cross the river safely. The route links several stations on the Léman Express and offers easy access to the southern slopes of the Mont Blanc range.

Free access all year. Difficulty: very easy. Distance: 40 km Bonneville-Sallanches, 3 h by bike. Best season: April to October. Fully accessible for visitors with reduced mobility, pushchairs, roller-skates and children's bikes. Free parking at the main access points.

via-ferrata-jallouvre-le-grand-bornand.body.what_is:
The via ferrata de la Tour du Jallouvre, at the Col de la Colombière near Le Grand-Bornand, is one of the most impressive in the Aravis range. With 1,100 m of cable and 590 m of elevation gain, it combines exposed traverses, metal rungs, dramatic crossings and a 16-metre suspension bridge - the Passerelle du Gypaète - which delivers serious thrills over the void.

Graded AD to D+ depending on the route taken, it is intended for participants in good physical condition with prior experience. The difficulty lies less in technical moves than in length and commitment: once across the bridge, you have to push on to the summit before descending via a hiking trail that can itself be tricky. An escape, the 'Sortie à Fred', shortens the route after the first section (around 500 m). The path starts at 1,410 m and climbs to 2,000 m, with a panorama over Le Chinaillon, the Aravis, the Pointe Percée and Mont Blanc.

Free access, late May to late October depending on snow. Mandatory equipment: harness, double lanyard with energy absorber, helmet and mountain boots. For beginners or supervised outings, the Bureau des Guides du Grand-Bornand offers group or private sessions with all equipment provided. Allow 30 min approach, 3-4 h on the route and 1 h for the descent.

via-ferrata-sixt-fer-a-cheval.body.what_is:
The via ferrata du Mont, at Sixt-Fer-à-Cheval, is a free-access equipped route that overlooks the Gorges des Tines in the heart of the classified Cirque du Fer-à-Cheval. With 800 m of cable and 150 m of elevation gain, it combines cables, rungs, footbridges and exposed passages along a south-facing limestone wall, secured by a continuous lifeline.

The route starts gently along the Vire de l'Ours, a comfortable ledge running below the cliff, before gaining height at the Barme aux Corbès and the Dalle des Paresseux. Highlights include the Passerelle de l'Ou Izès (a 15-metre suspension bridge, very airy), the Courte Échelle (an 8-metre inverted ladder) and several exposed traverses. Three escapes allow the route to be adapted to the participant's level.

What sets this via ferrata apart is its progressive difficulty (graded F to D depending on the section) and the exceptional views over the Pointe de Sales, the Frêtes du Grenier and the village of Sixt. Free access all year, weather permitting. Mandatory equipment: harness, double lanyard with energy absorber, helmet and hiking boots. Guided outings available with the Bureau des Guides de Sixt. Allow 20 min approach from the free Gorges des Tines car park and 20-25 min for the descent.

viarhona-haute-savoie-saint-gingolph-sey.name: ViaRhôna Haute-Savoie
viarhona-haute-savoie-saint-gingolph-sey.meta_description: 145 km from Saint-Gingolph (Lake Geneva) to Seyssel (Rhône) via Évian, Thonon, Cluses and Sallanches. EuroVelo 17, mix of greenways and shared roads.
viarhona-haute-savoie-saint-gingolph-sey.hero_alt: ViaRhôna EV17 EuroVelo greenway crossing Haute-Savoie from Saint-Gingolph on Lake Geneva to Seyssel on the Rhône
viarhona-haute-savoie-saint-gingolph-sey.body.what_is:
The ViaRhôna Haute-Savoie is the Haute-Savoie section of EuroVelo 17 (EV17), the international cycle route linking Lake Geneva to the Mediterranean over 815 km in total. According to the Conseil départemental, Haute-Savoie counts 145 km between Saint-Gingolph and Seyssel. The route is a mix of dedicated greenways (around 60%) and low-traffic shared roads (around 40%), fully signposted with the ViaRhôna logo and the EV17 EuroVelo symbol.

The main Haute-Savoie stages are: 1) Saint-Gingolph to Thonon-les-Bains (30 km via Évian-les-Bains, shared road and a 2 km greenway into Thonon); 2) Thonon to Chens-sur-Léman (26 km through the Bas-Chablais via the medieval village of Yvoire); 3) Chens to Annemasse, then south through Annecy, Cluses, Sallanches and Seyssel - connecting with the V61 'Léman au Mont-Blanc' route around Cluses.

Free access all year. Difficulty: easy. Distance: 145 km Haute-Savoie section, around 2-3 days by bike. Best season: April to October. Mostly accessible for visitors with reduced mobility on the greenway sections; shared roads require road-legal bikes. Free parking at the main waypoints.

villa-du-parc-annemasse.name: Villa du Parc
villa-du-parc-annemasse.meta_title: Villa du Parc Annemasse - Art centre - FREE - 2026
villa-du-parc-annemasse.meta_description: Contemporary art centre of national interest, labelled by the Ministry of Culture in 2020. Free for all. Wed-Sun 2 p.m. to 6 p.m. in the Parc Montessuit.
villa-du-parc-annemasse.hero_alt: Stone facade of the 1865 villa housing the Villa du Parc contemporary art centre of national interest in the heart of the Parc Montessuit at Annemasse
villa-du-parc-annemasse.body.what_is:
The Villa du Parc is one of the few contemporary art centres in Haute-Savoie to hold the 'Centre d'art contemporain d'intérêt national' label, awarded by the French Minister of Culture in 2020 (source CNAP - Centre national des arts plastiques). Set inside an 1865 villa in the heart of the Parc Montessuit, at 12 rue de Genève in Annemasse, it is a major cultural institution of the Greater Geneva area on the French side.

The programme features three to four temporary contemporary-art exhibitions a year, alongside artists' residencies, outreach activities for young audiences, talks and meet-the-artist events. The Villa du Parc defends an ambitious yet accessible contemporary art, with strong outreach work in local schools and a dedicated focus on emerging artists.

Free entry for all visitors. Season: year-round, closed between exhibitions. Hours: Wednesday to Sunday, 2 p.m. to 6 p.m. The Parc Montessuit setting makes the visit a pleasant outing, easily combined with a walk in the park. Accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (ground floor and exhibition rooms).

voie-verte-arve-cluses-thyez.name: Arve Riverside Walk Cluses-Thyez
voie-verte-arve-cluses-thyez.meta_description: 5 km family loop along the Arve between Cluses and Thyez, leading to a leisure lake at the end - flat, wheelchair-accessible greenway in Haute-Savoie.
voie-verte-arve-cluses-thyez.hero_alt: Local family greenway - Arve Riverside Walk Cluses-Thyez at Cluses, Haute-Savoie - 2 x 2.5 km one-way, 5 km loop, very easy
voie-verte-arve-cluses-thyez.body.what_is:
The Promenade du bord d'Arve Cluses-Thyez is a local family greenway running alongside the Arve river between Cluses and Thyez. 2 x 2.5 km one-way, or a 5 km loop if walked as a round trip, it offers a flat, wide riverside path that is fully accessible for wheelchairs, pushchairs, roller-skates and children's bikes. Allow 45 min one-way by bike or 1 h 30 on foot.

The greenway leads to the leisure base at Thyez, set around a small landscaped lake with a sandy beach, lifeguarded swimming in summer, children's play areas, a snack bar and sports pitches. The combination of riverside walk and family leisure park makes it a popular weekend destination for locals from the Faucigny valley.

Free access all year, no ticketing. Difficulty: very easy. Distance: 5 km loop. Best season: April to October. Free parking at both ends. Fully accessible for reduced-mobility visitors. Dogs on leads accepted, with care around the bathing area in summer.

voie-verte-lac-annecy-annecy.name: Lake Annecy Greenway
voie-verte-lac-annecy-annecy.meta_description: 33 km dedicated greenway on the western shore from Annecy to Marlens, on a former railway line. Flat, asphalted, wheelchair-accessible. 1.5 M passages a year.
voie-verte-lac-annecy-annecy.hero_alt: Lake Annecy Greenway on the former western-shore railway line with cyclists and pedestrians, Haute-Savoie
voie-verte-lac-annecy-annecy.body.what_is:
The Voie verte du Lac d'Annecy is the emblematic cycling and pedestrian route of Haute-Savoie: 33 km of dedicated path along the former Annecy-Albertville railway line, closed in 1966 and progressively converted from 1996 onward by the SILA (Syndicat Mixte du Lac d'Annecy). The route runs exclusively along the west shore of the lake, from Pâquier d'Annecy (45,8992° N, 6,1294° E) to Val de Chaise at Marlens, extended by the V62 cycle route to Albertville on the Savoie side.

Features: smooth 3.50 m wide surfacing, flat (cumulative elevation gain ~20 m), waymarked and maintained year-round. Accessible to everyone: cyclists, pedestrians, skaters, strollers, wheelchairs, children's bikes. The route passes through 10 lakeside communes: Annecy, Sevrier, Saint-Jorioz, Duingt, Doussard, Lathuile, Giez, Faverges, Saint-Ferréol, Val de Chaise. According to SILA, the route sees more than 1,500,000 passages a year on its busiest sections - making it the most heavily used voie verte in the French Alps, with peaks of 10,000 passages/day in summer.

Tour du Lac d'Annecy by bike: a full loop of 38 to 42 km, combining the voie verte on the west shore with the D909a shared road on the east shore. Free, open access year-round, 24/7. Free car parks at the main access points: Pâquier d'Annecy, Sevrier, Saint-Jorioz, Doussard, Faverges. 2025-2026 works: 8 widening phases over 15.3 km on the west side, traffic maintained but with occasional diversions.

SILA manages and maintains the 30 km between Sevrier (Beaurivage) and Val de Chaise. The route has three underpasses avoiding crossings with motor vehicles, plus protective barriers at certain intersections. The extension from Bredannaz to Val de Chaise cost close to €9.3 million (SILA, the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes Region, the Haute-Savoie Department, and the European Union). Major rest area: the former Bredannaz railway station (Doussard), equipped with toilets, a drinking water fountain and a picnic area with tables and benches, where the association Les Amis de Bredannaz displays vintage railway equipment including a locomotive from 1911.

The official tour-of-the-lake loop is given as 40 km by SILA. The east shore, developed in stages between 2010 and 2022, comprises 13.5 km of voie verte, 3.5 km of cycle lane and 3 km of shared roads: there is no dedicated cycling facility through Veyrier-du-Lac and Menthon-Saint-Bernard, and only a single lane is provided through Talloires (in the Angon → Talloires direction). Hillier overall, the east shore is not recommended for families with young children, who should choose the west shore instead.

voile-cercle-thonon-thonon.name: Thonon Sailing Club
voile-cercle-thonon-thonon.meta_description: Cercle de la Voile de Thonon (CVT) at the Port de Rives: historic French Lake Geneva sailing club, school for Optimist, dinghy and catamaran, regattas and courses.
voile-cercle-thonon-thonon.hero_alt: Lake Geneva sailing club and sailing school - Cercle de la Voile de Thonon at Thonon-les-Bains, Haute-Savoie 74
voile-cercle-thonon-thonon.body.what_is:
The Cercle de la Voile de Thonon (CVT) is the historic French sailing club of Lake Geneva, based at the Port de Rives in Thonon-les-Bains. A member of the French Sailing Federation (FFV), it hosts a sailing school, organises club regattas on the lake and runs courses and training programmes for both adults and children.

The CVT covers all main disciplines: Optimist for children (from age 7), dinghies for teens and adults (Laser, 420), sport catamarans (Hobie Cat) and racing. Five-day courses run throughout the summer season, with private lessons and equipment hire also available. All instructors are FFV-qualified.

2026 prices: adult courses EUR 200-400 depending on discipline, child courses available. Season: April to October. Hours: lessons and rentals daily 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. in season; out of season by appointment. Partially accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (adapted sailing on request).

wakepark-ponton-embarcadere-saint-jorioz.name: Ponton de l'Embarcadère Wakepark
wakepark-ponton-embarcadere-saint-jorioz.meta_description: Main boat-towed wakeboard spot on Lake Annecy: water-skiing, wakeboarding, wakesurfing, towed buoy. Flat water in the morning, boat waves in the afternoon.
wakepark-ponton-embarcadere-saint-jorioz.hero_alt: Boat-towed wakeboard and water-skiing - Ponton de l'Embarcadère Wakepark at Saint-Jorioz, Haute-Savoie 74
wakepark-ponton-embarcadere-saint-jorioz.body.what_is:
The Ponton de l'Embarcadère Wakepark is the main boat-towed wakeboard spot on Lake Annecy, set on the western shore at Saint-Jorioz. The central location, with easy access from the bay of Saint-Jorioz out to open water, makes it the reference site for towed water sports on the lake, with a fleet of boats dedicated to water-skiing and wakeboarding.

The school covers every boat-towed discipline: classic water-skiing, wakeboarding, wakesurfing (recent addition), wakeskate and towed buoy for families. The instructors are FFSN-qualified. The early morning flat water is particularly well suited to technical wakeboarding, while afternoon boat waves and a more relaxed pace work well for family outings.

2026 prices: adult EUR 30-50 per session depending on the discipline, children's sessions available. Season: May to September. Hours: daily 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. in summer. Partially accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (pontoon access).

wakepark-tna-cable-park-arenthon.name: TNA Cable Park
wakepark-tna-cable-park-arenthon.meta_description: TNA Cable Park at Arenthon: the only true cable wakeboard park in Haute-Savoie. Cable-towed wakeboarding on a lake, with consistently flat water and longer sessions than boat-towed riding.
wakepark-tna-cable-park-arenthon.hero_alt: Cable wakeboard park - the only true cable-park in Haute-Savoie - TNA Cable Park at Arenthon, Haute-Savoie 74
wakepark-tna-cable-park-arenthon.body.what_is:
The TNA Cable Park, at Arenthon, is the only true cable wakeboard park in Haute-Savoie. Riders are towed by a fixed overhead cable rather than by a boat, with two major advantages: the water is always flat, and sessions are significantly longer. The site is set on a lake at Arenthon and is equipped with modules for riders of every level.

Compared with the boat-towed spot at the Ponton de l'Embarcadère in Saint-Jorioz, the cable park offers a more affordable and more accessible alternative. A one-hour cable session typically allows several jumps and tricks, where a 15-minute boat tow only fits one or two runs. The cable park is therefore the go-to spot for progress-oriented wakeboarders looking for repetition.

2026 prices: adult EUR 25-40 per session, children's sessions available. Season: May to September. Hours: daily 10 a.m. to 7 p.m. in summer. Partially accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (poolside and bar areas).

vitam-neydens.body.what_is:
Vitam Neydens is a unique multi-space leisure centre in the Geneva region, 15 minutes from Geneva and 30 minutes from Annecy, run by UCPA since 2017. The site combines six distinct spaces in one location: water park (indoor + outdoor), Wellness & Spa, Sport & Fitness (climbing, squash, badminton, gym), indoor play area, shopping gallery and several restaurants.

The water park features a 25 m swimming pool, a 500 m² balneoform pool (indoor and outdoor by season) heated to 33°, a current river, a fun paddling pool and 9 slides. Climbing is the main feature on the sport side, with one of the largest indoor walls in the Geneva area: 1,500 m² of climbing surface.

The site's main draw is the outdoor balneoform pool (open June-September) — the main reason to come in summer. The Spa is the winter option par excellence. Daily access from €25-32 depending on the offer.
